about:
Founder/CEO of Atom an artificial bodies company founded by Apple and Neuralink leadership. Atom's mission is to develop artificial bodies as capable as natural bodies by bringing AI to the human body. We're starting with a breakthrough AI prosthetic arm Atom Touch for 100M people worldwide with limb loss or difference who are tragically-underserved by prosthetics today.

In 2016 I joined Shaan Puri and Furqan Rydhan as cofounder of second-generation Bebo (acquired Amazon). Bebo was a startup studio based in San Francisco CA funded by Michael and Xochi Birch founders of the original Bebo social network (acquired AOL). The new Bebo created several hit products the last of which was the leading esports tournament platform and Twitch streaming app.

In 2013 I founded Prime (#1 Health app App Store) with the mission to help families coordinate healthcare. More than 10 000 families used Prime to coordinate care for their children aging parents and partners with chronic conditions. Prime was the first and leading app to enable families to instantly sync & access all of their health records from 50 000+ clinics and hospitals across the US.

In 2010 I joined Disqus (YC S07 acquired Zeta Global) as employee #10 and led both Product and Community teams. Disqus is the #1 networked comments & community platform on the Internet reaching 1B+ users across 1M+ websites including CNN NFL NYT PBS Bloomberg and many more.

You can also follow me on Twitter: https://twitter.com/thetylerhayes

about:
Angie Muller is the CEO and co-founder of Attunement building the clinical memory layer for behavioral health.
 
Behavioral health data is fragmented across incompatible systems formats and workflows confusing to every AI system trying to operate on top of it. Attunement structures patient context at the source giving operators and agentic systems a reconciliation layer that enables effective use of AI in clinical settings.
 
Previously: partnerships at Stripe and NVIDIA across ML infrastructure synthetic data and data labeling. MSc Applied Neuroscience King's College London. BA Psychology Barnard College Columbia University. YC alumni.
company_description:
Behavioral health is the only field of medicine that still runs on narrative records written for human clinicians invisible to every system that needs to act on them
.
When records can't be read by machines operators lose revenue to documentation errors AI systems hallucinate without patient context and clinicians spend hours on work that shouldn't require them.

Attunement structures behavioral health records at the source turning fragmented narrative clinical data into structured longitudinal patient memory that operators and AI systems can actually use.

Backed by Y Combinator Ride Home Fund and Robot Ventures.
headline: Building Attunement. Making clinical records work for AI systems

current_position_description: Maipl Therapeutics specialize in addressing medical conditions related to inflammation pain and fibrosis and is developing radically different first-in-class medicines to treat endometriosis-related period pain and other diseases.
company_description: Current endometriosis therapies fail approximately 50% of patients and are constrained by severe hormonal side effects NSAID toxicity or the need for invasive surgery. Maipl is developing MA-4604 a validated non-hormonal receptor-targeted therapy that directly treats the core symptom of dysmenorrhea (debilitating pain) without systemic hormone suppression. To expedite market entry and reduce capital risk Maipl is executing a Primary Dysmenorrhea (PD)-first clinical strategy. By initially targeting severe cramping unlinked to underlying pelvic disease we bypass the complex regulatory and clinical bottlenecks typical of endometriosis trials. This targeted approach drastically reduces development time and costs positioning MA-4604 for potential commercialization within four years.
headline: Co-founder & CSO @ Maipl Therapeutics Bridging Academia & Industry Drug Development & Commercialization Translating Resear

about:
Dr. Homan returned to the rigorous demands of leading a start-up therapeutics company and co-founded MetasTx LLC with Drs. Somanath Shenoy and Brian Cummings because he was so impressed with the published results from their academic research and strongly believes they have identified a potential breakthrough drug that may significantly improve the armamentarium for treating metastatic cancer to greatly extend and improve patients' lives. Since formation in August 2021 MetasTx has made great progress and is poised to begin IND-enabling studies that will lead to the critical First-In-Human clinical studies. 
Dr. Homan has drawn upon his academic background in pharmacology as well as hands-on and senior leadership experience in a broad range of patient focused therapeutic categories in start-up and multi-billion dollar healthcare companies to build an extensive global network that he can call upon as needs arise. 
As Founder and CEO of biotech and medical device companies in USA and UK he has achieved successful marketing authorization commercialization and exits and has had extensive experience transitioning technology from academic labs to businesses including recent projects in neurodegenerative and psychiatric diseases. 
For Sterling Health and The Boots Company (BHI) he led growth of businesses in consumer-focused pharmaceuticals and has led commercialization of hundreds of successful products. At Sterling he rapidly progressed through clinical science marketing and sales to lead R&D regulatory and clinical affairs for Europe led global Analgesic GI and Skin Care portfolios and instituted innovative product development organizations and processes. 
Dr. Homan led all scientific medical regulatory and development functions for start-up BHI with teams in the UK France Germany Australia and Thailand. He was responsible for integration of all scientific medical and regulatory functions for two major acquisitions and development of partnerships in Europe U.S. India and Japan that built BHI to £500 million revenue and resulted in a £2 billion sale to Reckitt Benckiser in 2005. 
As Head of Corporate Development and Innovation for Boots plc he led institution of best practices internationally and coached Directors in value creation through innovation. 
He has served as a Director for Whitehorse Recovery Center NH and is a member of the Steering Committee for CUMC TRx Accelerator as well as a successful angel inveator. 
Dr. Homan has a PhD in Pharmacology from UGA and a MBA from NYU Stern.
current_position_description: Metastatic cancer (mCa) led to 10 million global deaths (2021) despite a $72B drug market (projected to grow to $138B by 2032). MetasTx initial therapeutic target is metastatic prostate cancer (mPCa). 3.2M US men were living with PCa in 2020 more than 227K had mPCa with median expected survival of 4.2 years. There is a significant unmet need for drugs to treat or prevent mPCa without depleting testosterone destroys quality of life. MetasTx intends to stop epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT) a crucial process for cancer metastasis by blocking PAK1 activation. Decades of research by Dr. Somanath Shenoy (co-Founder CSO) have focused on molecular and genetic mechanisms of cell functions critical to mCa and revealed high expression of PAK1 in prostate and breast cancers. A small molecule (IPA3) blocks PAK1 in vitro but is not effective in vivo. Collaborating with Dr. Brian Cummings (co-Founder Advisor) Shenoy demonstrated that a plasma-stable patented formulation of IPA3 inhibited growth of PCa xenografts metastasis to lung and bones as well as the destructive actions of PCa cells injected into bone. MetasTx currently is synthesizing compounds from a library designed by David Crich PhD Head of Chemistry (GRA and David Chu Eminent Scholar In Drug Design) and screening these compounds in studies led by Dr. Jackson (Jay) Gibbs PhD Head of Biology (formerly Sr. Director of Ca Research Merck & Astra Zeneca) to identify an optimized lead that will be scaled up for IND enabling studies leading to IND approval and First in Human (Phase 1 / 2) clinicals projected for completion by mid-2028. MetasTx will focus on discovery development and early-stage clinicals partner for later clinicals commercialize through licenses and exit through acquisition. A highly entrepreneurial team of experienced advisors support our vision to change the course of this terrible disease to improve the length and Quality of Life for patients with metastatic cancer.
headline: Unlocking the secrets of metastatic cancer

about:

He has a PhD in neuroscience from KU Leuven Belgium and an MBBS (equivalent to US MD) degree from Adesh Institute of Medical Sciences. He has over ten years of combined experience in systems neuroscience and machine learning research.

His mission is to create AI that can emulate human cognition and behavior and apply it to solve complex problems in medicine and healthcare. He has also pioneered several innovative AI algorithms such as a quality-of-life predictive model for cervical cancer patients and a stroke lesion detection system. He has published numerous peer-reviewed papers in prestigious journals and presented his work at international conferences.

Besides being a scientist and an entrepreneur he is also passionate about astronomy and astrophotography. He established the TARA Observatory (MPC identifier: V63) in Texas which contributes data for near-earth objects and captures stunning images of deep space. He believes that exploring the mysteries of the brain and the universe is a lifelong pursuit that enriches his personal and professional growth.

about:
Interdisciplinary Scientist turned serial entrepreneur. 
During my PhD it was bridging Biology Chemistry and Physics. 
During my Postdoc at Oxford it was connecting Biochemistry Genomics and Oncology.
As an academic Group Leader it was all about Machine Learning and Genomics to outsmart cancer. 

Now I am building Gordion Bio and developing a new class of 'Inescapable' cancer medicines. These are designed based on patients' molecular data and rely on simultaneous inhibition of primary targets and their escape mechanisms.
company_description:
Gordion Bioscience has built a proprietary patient-derived dependency map across 20 000 genes and 26 cancer types - translating real tumor biology into pipeline decisions.Standard preclinical models (CRISPR screens cell lines) systematically misrepresent the dependencies operating in patient tumors. 
Gordion's map is built entirely from tumor biopsies revealing what tumors actually depend on in human disease.

Platform applications: 
- Target validation distinguishing clinically translatable targets from cell-line artifacts. 
- Combination strategy — identifying co-dependencies that limit monotherapy response (KRAS programs being a prime example). 
- Bispecific design — functional co-dependency data from patient tumors not inferred cell line biology.

Traction: Novel target against MTAP-mutated solid tumors licensed to Kohde Pharma (2025) first IO bispecific outlicensed (2026).

Proprietary portfolio: currently focusing on building a portfolio of Bi and Tri-specific antibodies for PDAC CRC and NSCLC.
headline: CEO Gordion Bio Translating patient data into successful oncology medicines

about:
Michael Brown is the Founder of Cloudnine AI and the company is pioneering Deterministic Associative Context.
A new approach to AI infrastructure that replaces probabilistic guesswork with mathematically verifiable traceable certainty.

Unlike traditional AI systems that rely on semantic inference opaque reasoning and schema-heavy architectures Cloudnine AI enables organizations to generate AI-driven insights with full lineage deterministic proof and transparent reasoning. 

At the core of the platform is the Operational Data Engine (ODE) powered by the proprietary REL-1 associative engine. ODE creates a deterministic context layer that automatically maps relationships directly from contextual signals within the data itself — without predefined schemas semantic guessing prompt engineering or model retraining.
This architecture enables:
• 100% Traceability
Every data point retains complete lineage including source file row column and timestamp metadata enabling instant auditability and source verification.
• Non-Destructive Virtualization
ODE operates as a virtual associative layer across existing infrastructure. Data remains in place with zero duplication migration or modification.
• Automatic Associative Context
The platform dynamically connects related entities events and concepts directly from raw contextual cues without requiring rigid data models or manual relationship mapping.
• Zero-Shot Agentic Navigation
AI agents can explore and reason across data relationships with minimal prompting enabling more reliable autonomous workflows.
• Zero Model Training
REL-1 dynamically builds contextual relationships without expensive retraining cycles significantly reducing operational complexity and compute overhead.
Cloudnine AI is building the foundation for transparent auditable and trustworthy AI systems — where every answer can be traced back to its exact source of truth.
current_position_description:
Michael Brown is the Founder & CEO of Cloudnine AI where we are pioneering deterministic context architecture designed to move enterprise AI beyond probabilistic guesswork and hallucination-prone systems. Our work focuses on associative intelligence persistent memory and structured context frameworks that enable AI agents to operate with greater accuracy continuity and explainability across enterprise environments.
 
Michael is also involved in emerging financial and sustainability initiatives including green stablecoin infrastructure tokenizing Real-world assets and clean energy collaborations with industry and Tribal Government partners. His work bridges AI infrastructure energy healthcare and next-generation digital finance.
 
With a focus on practical innovation and scalable architecture Michael is building technologies that aim to redefine how enterprises manage intelligence memory and contextual reasoning in AI systems
Augmentation Not Automation: The approach focuses on augmenting human intelligence by providing clear contextualized inputs rather than fully automating decision-making with opaque AI models.
Privacy and Compliance by Design: The system is built with a privacy-by-design framework utilizing an anonymized token system and transparent data lineage to meet strict compliance and governance needs (e.g. in healthcare finance and government). Our research believes technology breaks down barriers leaving no one behind - serving the last billion.
company_description:
Cloudnine AI is pioneering deterministic contextual intelligence to eliminate AI hallucinations and establish a new foundation for trustworthy enterprise AI.

Unlike traditional AI systems that rely on probabilistic inference opaque reasoning and schema-heavy architectures Cloudnine AI enables organizations to generate verifiable intelligence with full lineage transparent reasoning and deterministic proof.

Powered by the proprietary REL-1 associative engine and the Operational Data Engine (ODE) Cloudnine AI automatically creates contextual relationships directly from enterprise data—without predefined schemas semantic guessing prompt engineering or model retraining.

Our platform delivers:

• Deterministic contextual intelligence with mathematical certainty
• 100% data lineage and traceability
• Non-destructive virtualization across existing infrastructure
• Automatic associative context generation
• Zero-shot agentic navigation
• Zero model training and reduced compute overhead

Cloudnine AI empowers enterprises to deploy AI they can trust—where every insight is explainable auditable and traceable to its exact source of truth.

Our mission is to build AI infrastructure that replaces probabilistic guesswork with deterministic intelligence enabling organizations to make faster safer and more reliable decisions at scale.

We believe AI should predict prevent and cure disease accelerate innovation and ensure that no one is left behind.

#AIForTheLastBillion

company_description:
L'Université Joseph KI-ZERBO est la doyenne et la plus grande des Universités du Burkina Faso. Née à la suite de l'accord de coopération signé à Paris le 24 avril 1961 entre la Haute-Volta et la France l'Université Joseph KI-ZERBO est la traduction de la volonté des autorités politiques d'alors de réaffirmer la souveraineté nationale en matière d'enseignement supérieur. Erigée d'abord en Centre d'Enseignement Supérieur de Ouagadougou (CESUP) par l'Ordonnance N° 69-024/PRES/ENJS du 5 Mai 1969 ce n'est qu'à partir du 1er avril 1974 que l'Université de Ouagadougou (ancienne dénomination) a porté son nom par Ordonnance N°74-031/PRES/EN du 19 avril 1974. Elle a connu plusieurs mutations (restructuration refondation …) au cours de son évolution.

L'Université Joseph KI-ZERBO se voit érigée en Établissement Public à caractère Scientifique Culturel et Technique (EPSCT) par Décret N°2000-469/PRES/PM/MESSRS du 6 Octobre 2000

L'Université Joseph KI-ZERBO est classée ces dernières années parmi les meilleures Universités d'Afrique francophone. Elle occupe une place de choix sur le plan international en termes de productions scientifiques de contribution des enseignants-chercheurs aux débats de leurs présence et participation dans les instances scientifiques (sociétés savantes projets et programmes de recherche international colloque et séminaires) etc.

En 2018 le budget voté de l'UJKZ est de 26 milliards 218 millions 800mille de F CFA. Ce budget représente 23 3% du budget du MESRSI et 44 8% du budget consacré à l'enseignement supérieur.

En 2018 la proportion du budget de l'UJKZ par poste de dépenses montre que 45 5% du budget est consacré pour les dépenses de fonctionnement 42 7% pour les dépenses du personnel et 12 0% pour l'investissement.

company_description:
Ergo is a biotech-based startup focused on the production of key whilst complex animal proteins by plant cell precision fermentation.
Plant cells are the most sophisticated biological systems in the universe. For many years we thought that the only way to take advantage of its complexity was through the cultivation of genetically modified plants on large surfaces. But now at Ergo Bioscience we have developed a platform that allows us to "program" plant cells with multiple simultaneous genetic modifications and simultaneously allow us to produce millions of them in bioreactors in the same way that millions of computer processors are produced in series. This is the revolution of plant cells!
To initiate the revolution of plant cells we have developed clones capable of expressing mammalian proteins reproducing their entire structure with the aim of taking plant foods to another organoleptic and nutritional level. We can produce bovine myoglobin for application in plant-based meat and bovine caseins for application in plant-based cheeses.
We want to change the world! and we believe we have one of the technologies that can positively contribute to this change.
headline: CEO & Co-Founder at ERGO Biotech Entrepreneur MBA Redefining Biotech for a Sustainable Future

about:
Hi I’m Joe! I’m the CEO of Orchid an AI-powered EHR platform founded by mental health professionals for mental health professionals to save them time money and frustration. 

I’ve walked a nonlinear path to get here: I’ve been an attorney an entrepreneur — even a mathematician historian and aspiring academic. After earning my JD from Yale Law School (‘15) I was a deployment strategist for Palantir where I helped solve the needs of Fortune 100 companies. I also founded a non-profit called The State Gun Law Project which applies data analytics to thorny legal issues and which was backed by Schmidt Futures. 

During my academic days I was invited to lecture on a variety of topics at Yale Harvard London School of Economics and the McCombs School of Business (UT-Austin). These days I'm fortunate to be presenting at Orchid-sponsored mental healthcare conferences across the country. 

All of my experiences have fueled my passion for solving issues that make a positive impact on lives. Want to connect? Or learn more about Orchid? Drop me a message anytime.

about:
As a Board-Certified Physician in Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation and the Director of Research at Pennsylvania Pain and Spine Institute I’ve dedicated my career to pain: diagnosing its roots treating the conditions causing it and understanding the experiences of patients in pain. 

Over my years in practice I’ve seen numerous patients who suffer not only from chronic pain but from coexisting depression and anxiety. As a PM&R doc my goal is to help patients break that pain-depression-anxiety cycle. 

I believe that access to quality mental healthcare is a key piece of that puzzle. 

That’s why I’m proud to be an advisor and investor of Orchid: an affordable all-in-one EHR platform that actually makes it easy for mental health professionals to grow their practice connect with patients and build their referral networks. Combining medical expertise with cutting-edge EHR tech can help create better longer-lasting outcomes for physicians mental health professionals and their patients. 

I’m excited about the impact our product is making on the mental healthcare landscape one session at a time. Follow Orchid on LinkedIn to learn more about our mission and feel free to message me here with any questions about becoming a member!

about:
I grew up in Iran and fell in love with entrepreneurship at age 13 when I made pinback buttons featuring Persian cartoon characters and sold them at school. Those early days inspired me to save up move to the U.S. and earn a Master’s in engineering. Along the way I created a deep tech solution to help cities manage their assets—now used by 400+ municipalities across US and Canada—and launched my first company by age 23. I’ve also lived in Istanbul and frequently relocated which taught me firsthand the power of finding welcoming communities in each new place. Fitness was always the only way I was able to find belonging.

When I moved to Austin a couple of years ago I struggled once again to join the right fitness and wellness communities. I realized these communities lacked the tools they needed to grow and get discovered—so we founded SweatPals to change that. Today I’m passionate about making health and fitness communities accessible creating that sense of belonging we all crave.

I love discussing startups health fitness and wellness community building tech and entrepreneurship—and I’ve shared my insights at TEDx Boston Globe and CBS. If any of these topics spark your interest feel free to reach out!

about:
I am the CEO and Co-founder of Ceretype Neuromedicine a radiologist neuroscience researcher and expert in psychiatric neuroimaging with significant broad leadership and management experience. My passion for improving the lives of patients with neuropsychiatric disorders using biologically-based tools has led to the translation of my lab's innovative and novel academic work into clinically useful solutions via Ceretype Neuromedicine Inc. I have led company spin-out (from Brigham and Women's Hospital/Harvard Medical School) and development including defining strategic vision business model development intellectual property portfolio creation (6 patents issued 3 pending) licensing fund-raising client engagement and project development/implementation. I am a collaborative leader with exceptional communication skills. 

Previously I have directed top neuroimaging research laboratories at Weill Cornell Medical College and Brigham and Women’s Hospital (BWH)/Harvard Medical School for a total of 25 years initiating and overseeing multiple interdisciplinary scientific projects (15 as Principal Co-Principal or Site Principal Investigator) many funded through the NIH. As Director of Functional and Molecular Neuroimaging I built the programmatic and physical infrastructure for fMRI at BWH including directing a $13M building project (obtained and directed the execution of a $6M NIH grant). about:
Healthcare should be about the people it serves. For decades however we've built systems that prioritize everything but the consumer. As the founder of Thatch I'm here to change that. 

Our mission is simple: to build a healthcare system people love. I believe we can fix the system by putting individuals back at the center: empowering them to choose the healthcare they need.

The healthcare industry is stuck in the past. In large part it's a relic of a WWII-era decision to tie healthcare to employment. Today employer healthcare costs are rising faster than they have in 15 years: the traditional "defined benefit" model of group health insurance is failing.

Thatch provides the solution enabling the shift to a "defined contribution" model for health benefits (ICHRA) doing for healthcare what 401(k) did for pensions. By decoupling the employer from the healthcare decision we give companies financial predictability and employees the power of choice of plans in the stable 25-million-member individual market of the ACA. 

We need healthcare that works for people. I believe that if we put the consumer at the center American healthcare can become the greatest healthcare system in the world.

If you're ready to rethink healthcare let's connect.

about:
I've spent four decades at the forefront of technological revolutions — from PCs the internet and cloud computing to cybersecurity and now healthcare. Each stage has been a step toward something larger: the convergence of biological and digital intelligence and the possibility of turning scarcity into abundance.

I've had the fortune of a career that feels like a journey across technological epochs — each shaping today's information economy while deepening my perspective. Some might see four decades of innovation as "a career well played." I see it as a dress rehearsal for the epoch we are entering now.

The revolutions of desktop computing client-server networking the internet digital identity and privacy cloud and encryption were never endpoints. They were essential stages in a larger arc: the emergence of intelligence as an abundant resource.

Time is the great arbiter of meaningful contribution. Today biological neurons and digital counterparts are converging to create capacities far beyond anything humanity has known. The challenge — and the opportunity — is to direct this intelligence not simply toward efficiency or profit but toward dissolving scarcity itself.

Scarcity has long defined the human struggle and in many ways drove us to evolve intelligence as a survival tool. Now as intelligence becomes abundant we can eradicate its harshest legacies and inequities — those impacting longevity coexistence and quality of life. A world of sustainable abundance intelligently applied could elevate the tenets of life and spirit while driving into obsolescence intolerance and the stark divide of "haves" and "have-nots."

My work now is dedicated to exploring and enabling this abundance: advising and building systems that harness intelligence to transform healthcare and extend across multiple vectors of human need. Through ventures like Catch Mobile Health Catch Digital and independent evangelism of an exciting and powerful toolset. The TIME Nexus Abundance framework evangelized within the Abundance Manifesto are the product of my own deep context gained through the good fortune of a fantastic journey and the brilliant evangelism of highly regarded experts.

We hold the keys. It is our imperative to apply this gift of abundant intelligence with wisdom and resolve — so that humanity may finally step beyond scarcity and into an era of sustainable flourishing.

about:
Vassilios Papadopoulos DPharm PhD DSc (hon) was named dean of the USC School of Pharmacy—now known as the USC Alfred E. Mann School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences—in fall 2016. Before joining USC he served as executive director and chief scientific officer of the Research Institute of McGill University Health Centre in Montreal and as faculty department chair and director of the Biomedical Graduate Research Organization at Georgetown University Medical Centre.

A noted scientist and innovative leader Papadopoulos has published more than 350 papers holds numerous patents serves on many national and international advisory committees and boards of biotechnology companies as well as a consultant for the pharmaceutical industry. He is an elected foreign member of the National Academies of Medicine and Pharmacy in France fellow of the American Association for the Advancement of Science and fellow of the Canadian Academy of Health Sciences.

Papadopoulos’ research focuses on understanding the cellular and molecular mechanisms responsible for the initiation and maintenance of steroid biosynthesis in the adrenal gonads and brain in health and disease. He also examines the regulation of steroid biosynthesis intracellular compartmentalization and homeostasis by hormones chemicals drugs natural products and environmental factors. His research has direct applications in reproduction and development aging cancer and neurological and neuropsychiatric disorders.

about:
Above all: Peter DePaulo has taken a vow to put the well-being of others above his own life. This is his oath to make a positive impact in his time here on earth. 

Peter DePaulo is an energetic Founder Writer Speaker and Software Engineer living in the SF Bay Area. Growing up surfing in California the spark for Innovation was ignited at a young age. By the time he was accepted to UC San Diego's Biochemistry program (#1 in the world for Biochemistry and surf) at 16 years old he was a Junior in college.

After graduating his first startup was acqui-hired into an EduTech startup. They were in the FA13 cohort of Stanford's StartX program for entrepreneurs. At Stanford Peter led cross-functional teams of professors and staff of the institution. He presented on EduTech and Improving Student Outcomes at the Stanford GSB.

After helping his friend found Viking Education (later sold to Thinkful) he went deeper into Software Engineering. He was an early Ethereum blockchain developer co-founded a Behavioral Economics collective of Software Engineers wrote code for wellness at MINDBODY and led projects in oncology data in precision medicine at Diaceutics partnering with Intel Pfizer Merck and Roche to name a few.

He presented the team's AI clustering models of cancer biomarkers at UCSF Medical School Harvard and to the BROAD institute. It was at this time that Tyler Capps invited him to write articles on Behavior for a small project that would later be called Sophonaut.

Next was NEAR Protocol the developer friendly blockchain. He took the helm of Developer Relations. The cryptocurrency he helped bring to market was at its peak of a $20 billion dollar market cap when he raised $1.5 million to found Satori the NFT company for helping artists. This is when he faced the greatest challenge of his career: burnout.

He left his company to a friend and went into a deep recovery period. He realized that his career was one relentless pursuit after another: sprinting and burning out. Having written about Behavior for prior three years he dove full time into improving the lives of others. Now he serves as co-founder and Head Maverick of Sophonaut! Helping people avoid the vicious cycle that the world cheers on.

Outside the office he meditates surfs makes music plays games and spends quality time with his wife Kiran and dog Bosco.

I’m a former U.S. Army Combat Medic turned biotech entrepreneur driven by a mission to decentralize and democratize global healthcare. Today I lead IndyGeneUS Biosciences a biofintech company unlocking the power of African genomic data AI and blockchain to revolutionize precision medicine. We’re building one of the world’s largest diverse biobanks and launching tools like the DNA token to ethically compensate individuals for their data. My focus is simple: transform the future of medicine—and ensure it includes everyone.
company_description:
IndyGeneUS Bio is a BioFinTech company building the largest and most inclusive genomic database in the world transforming underrepresented genomic data into Biological Capital. We are the BlackRock of Genomics bringing equity discovery and economic participation to communities that have been excluded from the genomic revolution.

With a rapidly expanding 700 000-plus whole-blood sample dataset and a global clinico-genomic ecosystem we are redefining how biological data is collected protected valued and reinvested back into the people it comes from. Our vision is to reach 1 million genomes and bring representation to the six billion people missing from today’s datasets.

Our infrastructure includes advanced analytics sovereign-ready governance trusted community sampling frameworks and a global network of researchers and health systems. By pairing community-centered stewardship with deep clinico-genomic insights we uncover drug targets disease pathways and innovations that legacy datasets have overlooked.

This is diversity as alpha and BioFinTech as a new category that enables secure consent-driven economic participation. As global pharma loses more than two hundred billion dollars annually due to incomplete data our model strengthens discovery and increases return on investment.

IndyGeneUS Bio is led by Founder and CEO Yusuf Henriques a former FDA regulator U.S. Army combat medic and award-winning genomics innovator. As we expand our global footprint and scale high-value relationships across pharma and biotech we remain focused on building the world’s most trusted and most equitable clinico-genomic ecosystem creating better health outcomes and generational equity through Biological Capital.

about:
When I moved to D.C. twenty years ago for law school I never thought I’d wind up a co-founder of a group coaching platform that builds human skills and combats the loneliness epidemic. But the culture of Big Law demanded it – and as my serial entrepreneur mother taught me if you see a problem go fix it.

My legal career ranged from Am100 law firms Pillsbury and Cooley LLP to a stint as senior counsel for GridPoint in the wild wild west of the tech startup world to building a legal team from the ground up at Hewlett Packard. As is the story for so many of us trying to balance a career and caregiving responsibilities the birth of my daughter changed everything.

It wasn’t the identity shift so many of us experience during the transition to parenthood. It wasn’t the lack of perks at work. I realized I didn’t have the skills to adequately care for and advocate for myself while balancing my two demanding jobs lawyer and mother. Access to and help building those skills just didn’t exist in my personal and professional worlds. How do you ask for help when you’ve been trained to be perfect and “do it all”? So for the first time I felt like I was failing…and THAT was an incredibly lonely feeling.

So I pivoted. I became a leadership coach and worked in talent management for Hogan Lovells. Hearing the exit interviews from dozens of successful lawyers made me realize these feelings were incredibly commonplace. And the “it is what it is” mentality echoed around the “juggle” and the impact it has on the health and well-being of working parents and caregivers infuriated me. It wasn’t an individual’s personal problem it was a structural widespread systemic issue that needed a new solution. 

Enter: Keep Company. Keep Company started with parents and caregivers. We know that once you step into a caregiving role you never really step back out be that caring for a newborn an older child a spouse with a serious illness or an aging parent. Keep Company combines a curriculum rooted in social-emotional learning and behavioral science with expert coaching and a community of similarly situated peers that really get what you’re going through.

We work with employers who want to build more effective and productive teams and while we started by supporting parents and caregivers to mitigate burnout and avoid regrettable attrition…guess what? All employees are struggling to keep up the pace of change in this new era of work. At Keep Company they can learn practice and apply the human skills necessary to succeed – at work and in life.
company_description:
Keep Company is the modern mentoring and coaching platform that helps organizations rebuild the human layer of work.

Our patented matching technology automated scheduling and built-in impact reporting make it easy to launch scale and measure high impact people programs that strengthen connection resilience and performance.

Alongside our platform we deliver full-service signature programs for your most important employee groups—including Ready to Launch for incoming associates Accelerate for early-career talent and Parents & Caregivers highlighted by our new national report with NAMI on the needs of working caregivers.

Whether you’re looking to scale and strengthen your existing mentoring and coaching programs or partner with our expert facilitators Keep Company helps your employees feel connected and supported—and gives organizations the infrastructure to develop talent with less admin and more impact.
headline: Co-Founder & CEO @ Keep Company Helping companies build the human skills for a new era of work

about:
Call it "I moved around a lot when I was little" or a middle-child thing but I'm a resourcer. I'm always asking: What are the tools and tactics? What's the goal and how do we get there? That served me well—first in corporate comms then building brands at high-growth startups. And then I had my first child…

Suddenly all my old strategies didn't work. Where was the strategy? We're all just survival-moding this thing?! 

I started looking for "tools and tactics" in products services and people. I devoured work-life balance books and self-care apps and found endless "right answers" on how to do and have it all. None of them really helped.

So how do we keep ourselves steady and our people engaged amidst change? Turns out it's less about playbooks and more about connection. Less about knowing exactly what the path looks like and more about having the right people to help you get there.

This is why I co-founded Keep Company.

Loneliness makes every hard thing we navigate harder. Human connection is an antidote to so much—it's a balm and a profound need. And yet it's harder to find than ever.

The human layer of work—mentorship peer learning organic relationships connection—used to happen naturally. That's gone replaced by tracked changes onboarding via an AI agent and async collaboration that moves fast but comes with a cost we can't keep ignoring.

Employees feel alone. Managers are stretched thin trying to bridge gaps relationships used to cover. New talent is confused their bosses are frustrated. Organizations are quietly bleeding money through disengagement turnover and underdeveloped talent.

There's no software to fix this. Until now.

Connection is the most underestimated competitive advantage of our time. I'm proud to work on making the power of human connection measurable scalable and undeniable.

When I'm not having big ideas about the loneliness epidemic—or big feelings about brand fonts—I'm trying (often failing but always trying again!) to practice what we preach to our members every day. I'm originally from Venezuela and live in Washington DC with my husband and two boys. Currently I'm learning how to play Pokemon… lovingly but against my will.

about:
For the past two decades my research has focused on developing innovative solutions to enhance accessibility and effectiveness in mental health care. My journey began in 2007 when I immigrated to Canada and joined the Mood Research Lab at Queen’s University. Witnessing the challenges immigrants faced in accessing mental health care inspired me to pioneer online psychotherapy solutions tailored to diverse populations. This journey reached a significant milestone with the creation of OPTT (Online Psychotherapy Tool) a secure platform integrating data-driven practices and AI algorithms recognized globally for its impact on mental health care delivery.

Joining the Centre for Addiction and Mental Health (CAMH) and later Queen’s University I established an academic online psychotherapy lab supervising a diverse team of trainees. company_description:
At Traini Inc. we are building the emotional and behavioral intelligence layer for non-human beings.

Traini is backed by investors and industry leaders including FutureX Capital Banyan Tree Silver Capital NYX Ventures Strating Gate Fund Jade Capital Tao Foundation and Valkyrie Fund I LP senior leaders from NVIDIA & Anthropic the co-founder of Xiaomi.

Traini is an AI-native company focused on enabling true understanding and communication between humans and animals — starting with dogs.

By leveraging multimodal real-time inference we go beyond simple signal detection to interpret emotion intent and behavior in context. Our goal is not just to recognize what pets do but to understand why they do it.

We are also developing the world’s first cognitive wearable device for pets — ushering in a new era of pet-human connection. This system combines real-time sensing AI reasoning and long-term behavioral memory to capture the multidimensional nature of animal emotions.

At the core of Traini is a belief:
AI should not only increase intelligence — it should enable empathy.

Our technology stack includes:
• PetGPT — a conversational AI that understands both pets and humans enabling natural intent-level interaction
• Real-time emotion & behavior translation powered by multimodal AI
• Autonomous agents that assist pet parents and businesses across the pet care ecosystem
• Data-driven personalization for food health and behavioral decisions

We are building a future where pets are not just observed — but understood and eventually empowered to participate in decisions that affect their lives.

Today Traini serves users in 189 countries and continues to expand its AI capabilities across consumer and enterprise applications.

Careers: trainipet@gmail.com

about:
I've spent most of my life chasing something bigger than titles or milestones: meaning.

I'm a Gulf War Navy veteran and healthcare technologist not by accident but by design. The Navy is where I learned to build systems. Losing my father to a preventable medication side effect is why I chose to build them in healthcare.

That moment became my North Star.

Today I'm the Founder and CEO of Naviday Health where we're building the intelligence layer between consumer health data and clinical care. Our first product LUCI (Lifestyle Understanding Care Intelligence) is an AI-powered menopause companion that transforms the data women generate daily from wearables home labs and connected devices into clinically meaningful insights.

I believe health begins with trust: trust in ourselves the data we generate and the systems meant to care for us. My work is about helping people reclaim their role in the healthcare journey using technology as a bridge not a barrier.

Over the past two decades I've worked in tech across multiple industries participated in two IPOs and studied global healthcare leadership at Harvard Medical School. I'm also the author of "How to Catch a Turtle: An Introduction to Mindfulness" and host of the MenInPause podcast exploring how men can better support the women in their lives through midlife health transitions.

Building toward a future where the data people generate daily actually informs the care they receive.

company_description:
We believe healthcare should begin with the person—not the system.

Naviday Health is building AI-guided care plans that help individuals navigate complex health transitions starting with menopause. While traditional systems wait for symptoms to escalate we capture daily lived experiences and turn them into timely personalized care.

This isn't about collecting more data—it's about making the right data matter.

We combine AI-driven insights blockchain-secured Consumer Health Records (CHR) and strategic partnerships with world-class institutions to deliver care that adapts in real-time. The result is a platform that shifts healthcare from episodic intervention to continuous personalized support for both the individual and the provider.

We're not here to optimize the old system.
We're here to build what comes next.

PinkSteady is a digital health company focused on balance stability and confidence as people age.

We use everyday devices like smartphones to help adults understand their balance over time and support steadiness through simple at-home experiences. Our approach goes beyond detecting problems after they occur by helping people recognize and respond to changes early.

PinkSteady is built for individuals and families who want to stay independent as well as caregivers and organizations supporting aging adults.

Our science is built on pink noise — the natural rhythm found in healthy brain activity heartbeats and movement. Backed by the NIH the Department of Defense and leading senior living partners we transform fall prevention from reactive detection to proactive stability enhancement.

With 24/7 monitoring AI-driven early detection and pink-noise–based interventions PinkSteady empowers seniors families care facilities and insurers with measurable outcomes:

 Stronger stability & confidence for seniors

 Peace of mind for families

 Reduced costs & liability for facilities

 Proven ROI for insurers and payers

Falls are the leading cause of lost independence. We’re changing that story.
Join us in building a safer more confident future for aging.

Taking the whole healthcare professional experience online.

about:
A Gallup leadership assessment once informed me “you are part artist and part scientist - and comfortable working at the intersection of both”. 

Wait--does Gallup have spies? Are they Deep State??

Part geek and part bohemian I'm just as likely to begin a long email or blog post at 3A as I am to be down a rabbit hole about the mitochondrial DNA of invasive raccoons (true story). 

The takeaway here: I sleep with my Macbook. 

Topline: 

- Writer + master communicator. Folks often think my education is Comms it’s actually English Lit & Creative Writing. 
- Warm but rarely measured. Shoot from the hip and keep my throat chakra open. And yes - get to know me well enough and I'll probably mention a chakra. Or two.
- I take my work very seriously rarely take myself seriously. Cheek knows tongue. 
- When I believe in something I'm like a collie with a bone. 


Life and work can be summed up under two themes for me: STORY + STRATEGY. 

As a storyteller I've been fortunate to enjoy parallel careers as a writer/creative and an executive/clinical administrator in Assisted Reproductive Technology. Equally passionate about both and despite Frostian posits about two roads diverged in a wood I've never been able to take just one.

My overarching mission is to optimize the life experience by engineering narratives + creating solutions that fuel profound connection. I'm open to all this can mean. 

I refer to myself as a "baby-making enthusiast" + "family-building evangelist" and am particularly passionate about the mission of affording all women better family-forming options. 
 
 . Been in Assisted Reproductive Technology since 2008 work that's made me deeply aware of the intimate relationship between educating women about their remarkable biology and empowering them to live well in all areas of life. 

 . As a creative I’ve been privileged to work in the advertising tech and production spaces with brands such as 310 Nutrition Pax Labs Koi Story Studios Walden Media Hallmark WW Shea Moisture and Nike. 

I founded CLAIRE Fertility to address the delta between clinical fertility treatment and women’s comprehensive wellness. 

Then I co-founded Snatch Health to address the unfortunate side effects of innovating as a woman for women.

I’m embarrassingly zealous about FemTech and all the brilliant solutions it’s birthing to improve women's health.

Always available to chat about ways I can work with others to improve clinical cultural and societal experiences for women + families.

Ping me:
ashleigh@snatchhealth.com

about:
I’m driven by a passion for building innovative brands products and digital experiences that improve the health and well-being of people everywhere while generating concrete business value. 

My career has taken me on a dynamic journey across industries from supplement manufacturing to tech innovation and global consumer goods always with a focus on enhancing lives through meaningful impactful solutions.

I began my career at the largest softgel supplement manufacturer in North America where I worked closely with physicians to create private label products. This hands-on experience in the health sector laid the foundation for my future work. After earning my MBA I ventured into the world of education and then joined Accenture Ventures in Silicon Valley during the height of corporate innovation. There I connected cutting-edge tech startups with Accenture’s global clients to forge partnerships and investment opportunities.

I then spent six years at Nestlé leading digital innovation efforts for brands like Nescafé Nestlé Skin Health KitKat Milo and Nespresso. My work focused on designing digital experiences that empowered consumers to live healthier more fulfilling lives. I also was an Entrepreneur-in-Residence at a venture studio working with IKEA Citi and Novozymes to ideate and validate new business ideas.

Most recently I developed and brought to market Minu a dermatologist-designed mineral suncare brand alongside a talented team of Neutrogena veterans and a Stanford dermatologist. Minu has quickly become a favorite among beauty editors dermatologists and consumers now available nationwide at Credo Beauty one of the largest clean beauty retailers. Coverage in top outlets like Forbes The Cut Beauty Independent Who What Wear and Marie Claire has helped bring the brand’s mission to life: creating exceptional sun care that feels like luxurious skincare.

As a mother of two young children I’m fueled by a deep commitment to building a better world for future generations. I’m also the proud daughter of refugees whose resilience and work ethic continue to inspire me to push boundaries take risks and never stop striving for excellence to create impactful change in health wellness and beyond.

about: I am a cardiologist with 18 years of experience in clinical practice. As the only doctor in my family I've been faced with the challenge of helping my aging parents try to navigate their healthcare needs in the small rural community in Vermont where they retired. As a specialist in the Pacific Northwest I have seen firsthand the disparity in access to specialty procedural healthcare experienced by people living in rural and underserved regions in the US and worldwide. To address this geographic disparity my team and I founded Dopl with a mission to improve access to specialty healthcare procedures (diagnostic and therapeutic) using a novel software platform to allow remote specialists to perform procedures over distance using the internet and a robot. Our goal is to allow patients to receive world-class healthcare regardless of their zip code.
company_description: Our vision is to enable a world where everyone has convenient access to high-quality diagnostic exams and interventional procedures so that they can live longer healthier lives. To achieve this vision we're using the latest advances in AI robotics and networking to break down access barriers starting in historically underserved communities.
headline: Professor University of Washington Co-Founder & COO of dopl technologies

about:
Becoming a mum changed everything for me—especially how I think about the food we feed our children. I came to realize that what nourishes our kids should also nourish our communities.

That’s why Bebe Bitez is grounded in Farm to School values. We partner with local farms to deliver fresh nutrient-rich meals to preschools and childcare centers—meals made with ingredients you’d be proud to serve at home. By sourcing locally we support small farmers reduce our environmental impact and help children build a lifelong connection to real food.

Bebe Bitez isn’t just a food company. It’s a mission-driven movement to transform how we feed the next generation. We're here to prevent diet-related illness in children by improving food access advocating for higher standards in school nutrition and bringing transparency and integrity back into what ends up on kids’ plates.

When we prioritize fresh local and thoughtfully prepared meals we’re doing more than feeding kids—we’re investing in their health their future and the future of our local food systems.
company_description: Bebe Bitez is a Bay Area-based baby food company committed to ensuring that every child regardless of demographic has access to the best quality food from the moment they start solids. We provide CACFP-compliant nutritious meals for preschools daycares and direct-to-consumer while also advocating against harmful ingredients in children’s products and pushing for better access to and education about high-quality ingredients. Our focus is on food as medicine and creating a healthier future for all children.

company_description:
Truentity Health turns local pharmacies into the operating system for chronic care in Medicare and Medicaid populations. Our AI-powered platform enables pharmacists to deliver reimbursable care programs like Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) Chronic Care Management (CCM) and Transitional Care Management (TCM) improving outcomes while creating sustainable clinical revenue.

By surfacing risk guiding action and automating workflows our intelligence layer helps pharmacies scale high-quality care without adding staff. With proven results including double-digit reductions in blood pressure and meaningful A1C improvements Truentity is redefining how chronic care is delivered through America’s most trusted community healthcare touchpoints.

Truentity works with independent pharmacies and health partners across the United States including collaborations with state-led Rural Health Transformation Program (RHTP) initiatives focused on expanding access strengthening rural care infrastructure and improving outcomes in underserved communities. These efforts enable local pharmacies to operate as clinical care hubs that support prevention workforce capacity and technology-enabled chronic disease management.

about:
After a brief flirtation with screenwriting and film production I was a Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) executive for over 15 years. My outsourcing career has been deeply rewarding it is the only industry where a non-core yet essential support function can be transformed by transferring operating ownership. The customer’s cost center becomes the provider’s profit center enabling greater investment in innovation through technology process improvement and change management. Throughout my time in the space I had the privilege of serving in operational delivery analytics and sales roles. The engagements I was fortunate to participate in provided tremendous value to clients in a variety of industries and was very fulfilling for me.

I am now focused on empowering healthcare patients. In December of 2014 my wife and I welcomed twins into the world. They were 15 weeks premature however and the many health issues associated with prematurity left me feeling helpless for the first time. The three-month NICU journey taught me much about the importance of personal empowerment a topic I thought I was an expert on. The reality is I wasn’t even close to understanding the importance of it until I faced the most critical situation and had no personal expertise to rely on.

This experience has inspired me to launch Nicolette. Our mission is to empower patients by making complex health data consumable for the layperson. Our first product is called NicoBoard a personal dashboard for NICU parents. I am proud and honored to have the opportunity to empower new parents to be strong advocates for their children’s health.
company_description:
Patient Empowerment. Provider Partnership.

Nicolette's first product is NicoBoard a parent-facing dashboard application that maps a premature baby's plan of care and tracks progress in a way that parents can understand. The application visually translates critical health data enabling parents to be empowered members of their baby's healthcare team. Hospitals will provide a specialized tablet to parents that contains the app and parents can also download the app to their personal devices.

about:
I spent years in medicine research and public health before I saw the real problem clearly: healthcare doesn't have a care problem. It has an operations problem.
Physicians are burning out not because they don't love the work but because the systems around them are failing. Two hours of documentation for every hour of patient care. Revenue leaking through undercoding missed care gaps and broken referral loops. Quality bonuses are left uncollected because no one has time to track them.
I built DocPro Health to change that. We're an AI-powered operating system that handles charting revenue optimization care gap closure and value-based care performance so physicians can focus on what they trained to do.
My background spans clinical medicine cancer biology research at Northwestern and UChicago and health strategy at NYU. That combination of understanding both the science and the business of healthcare is what shapes everything we build at DocPro.
We're working with practices IPAs and MSOs across the country. If your organization is ready to run smarter I'd welcome the conversation.
www.doc.pro tanwir@doc.pro

Designed for independent practices IPAs and value-based care organizations DocPro transforms fragmented workflows into a unified data-driven performance engine. As the CEO and founder of PONS and INESS I am responsible for strategic business development and partnerships across the US and APAC region as well as empowering healthcare professionals by optimizing the time cost and effort spent in providing remote medical imaging.

I have faced and overcome many challenges and crises in my professional journey from surviving two coup attempts in two different countries to adapting to a financial crisis that destroyed 50% of the economy in a developing country. I never gave up and managed to sustain our teams in those countries while always trying new things and learning from failures. I believe that resilience is the key factor to success and that dreams are the fuel for innovation. company_description:
Our mission at PONS is to optimize access to healthcare and improve the quality of life for all individuals irrespective of their location race or income. We aim to ensure that no one loses their loved ones prematurely due to preventable diseases. 

To achieve this we enhance the capabilities of widely available ultrasound devices by integrating them with cloud-connected artificial intelligence. This innovation addresses the common limitations of imaging that hinder early disease detection and preventive screening. Through software advancements we unlock a new era of proactive personalized care enabling healthcare providers to deliver effective and timely diagnostics.

PONS` device-agnostic patented software and AI models to optimize ultrasound imaging quality augment tissue contrast and reduce signal noise while revealing anatomical and pathological features structural delineations and microenvironmental context that remain undetectable with conventional ultrasound systems. PONS` patented technology can implement digital contrast without needing chemical contrast injected into the patients revailing the surrounding on the earliest stages Our proprietary deep learning algorithms quantitatively analyze temporal variations in morphological and spatial parameters—including tissue geometry volumetric dimensions location surrounding and positional dynamics—to generate AI-powered diagnostic risk assessment reports.

I believe every person deserves to feel inspired and empowered in their own learning journey. For over 15 years I’ve worked at the intersection of education technology community design and storytelling building systems that center engagement agency and belonging.

I’m the co-founder of The Future Frequency (FQ) a personalized learning ecosystem designed to help learners own their growth and for guardians to grow alongside them. At the heart of FQ is LearningFREQUENCY (LF) a platform that visualizes how individuals engage process and evolve transforming insights into living data and verifiable credentials that stay with the learner for life. Alongside LF I co-created Gatekeepers to Guardians (G2G) a personalized LMS supporting the adults guiding learners equipping parents educators and mentors with tools to support deep aligned learning.

I’m a principal at Skyway a global initiative of the Transforming Education Exchange (TEE) and the Learning Economy Foundation. 

My Seventh Level Engagement Framework developed during my Master’s in Curriculum and Instruction and based on research by Bangert-Drowns and Pyke has been taught in programs at Yale Howard University and Emerson and shared with more than 1 400 professors in partnership with HubSpot. My book The Seventh Level became the foundation for CatalystU where I’ve partnered with organizations like HubSpot and American Express to help individuals apply this framework to build intentional engaged communities.

From 2012 onward I co-founded and led CatalystCreativ with Tony Hsieh as part of the 350 million dollar Downtown Las Vegas revitalization project. Through CatalystCreativ I worked with clients such as Google Coca-Cola NPR Dell W Hotels The Raiders and The Nature Conservancy on brand development community design and experiential strategy as well as developed a Cannes Lion award winning campaign for WildAid. 

I’m also the co-founder of Runway Health and have been an active advisor and angel investor in more than 30 companies including HubSpot SEED Clay Kiddie Kredit and Solsten.

I’ve been featured in TIME WSJ Fast Company Forbes and Entrepreneur and have spoken at TEDx INBOUND Summit the UN and Founders Forum. I continue to explore how learning ecosystems can unlock purpose and potential for every learner and every guardian.
company_description:
The Future FQ is an intelligent engagement engine that understands and elevates human attention. It helps organizations transform passive consumption into meaningful participation by personalizing how people learn interact and respond across any content or system.

The Future FQ analyzes emotional cognitive and motivational patterns and adapts experiences in real time. This creates dynamic pathways that feel personal relevant and alive activating the human layer of engagement that traditional systems overlook.

The Future FQ integrates seamlessly into LMS and CMS environments and supports partners across education enterprise media gaming and workforce development. Its foundation is rooted in engagement science and the Seventh Level Framework developed by our founder during her Master’s in Curriculum and Instruction.

The Future FQ serves both individuals and organizations. It enhances user experience while providing deep insights into how people pay attention where they disconnect and what strengthens motivation confidence and retention.

Our mission is to unlock the human frequency of engagement. The Future FQ brings intelligence care and personalization to every interaction empowering people to feel seen supported and capable in their own learning and growth

current_position_description:
Building the world's first and only open-source API and platform for healthcare data exchange. 

Metriport is open-source because we believe that no one should have to work with blackbox solutions especially when dealing with something as critical as healthcare data where transparency and security are absolutely key.

For example given how important CCDA-FHIR conversion is in healthcare you'd think it's a fairly solved problem… nope. There are no standards here with only mediocre black-box solutions you can't trust. This is why we open-sourced our FHIR converter: https://www.metriport.com/fhir-converter

Whether you're looking to exchange data in FHIR R4 with all the major hospitals in the US or get your clinicians medical record summaries before patient encounters Metriport enables leaders in healthcare to innovate through a fully customizable solution that can be plugged directly into existing workflows: https://www.metriport.com/customers/tono-health

If you're interested in learning more about Metriport and the internet for healthcare data we're building check out our Hacker News post (https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=40456064) or grab some time with me directly: https://calendly.com/dimagoncharov/30m
company_description: Metriport is an open-source platform that makes it easy for healthcare organizations to access and exchange medical data for their patients.

about:
To keep global temperature rise below 1.5 °C we need to remove billions of tons of carbon dioxide each year. There’s growing consensus that it will be virtually impossible to achieve this without carbon removal to mitigate emissions from hard‐to‐abate industries and draw down legacy emissions.

As Head of Strategic Initiatives at Heirloom I’m working to commercialize one of the few low-cost carbon removal technologies capable of reaching billion-ton scale. Heirloom’s approach combines the best of two well-established technologies – carbon mineralization and direct air capture (DAC) – to capture carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere and then permanently and safely store it underground.

Please include a personal note with LinkedIn connection requests.
current_position_description: Heirloom combines the best of two well-established CO2 removal technologies – carbon mineralization and direct air capture (DAC) – to build a truly scalable low-cost carbon removal solution. We use limestone one of the world’s most abundant and inexpensive minerals to capture CO2 directly from the atmosphere and then permanently and safely store it underground.
company_description:
Mitigating the worst impacts of climate change by developing the world's most cost-effective DAC technology to permanently remove CO2 from the atmosphere.

Our technology rapidly accelerates the natural processes that enable limestone to absorb carbon dioxide from the air from a timespan of years to days.

In 2023 Heirloom established the first and only Direct Air Capture facility in North America that permanently captures CO2. Our customers are the world's biggest buyers of carbon removal including Microsoft JP Morgan McKinsey Stripe Shopify and more.
headline: Head of Strategic Initiatives @ Heirloom Climate DAC

My journey into plant-based food began 8 years ago when I transitioned to an alkaline vegan diet to manage several health conditions. After quitting animal-products cold turkey (pun intended) I experienced strong food cravings but couldn’t find meat alternatives on the market that worked with my whole food plant based diet. 

As a fermentation hobbyist I discovered that microbes and fungi were often more responsible for the flavors and textures that I missed than the animal-based ingredients and allergens I’d given up. I’m also formally trained in mushroom cultivation culinary arts and plant-based nutrition.

headline: Co-Founder & CPO @ Handl Health

about:
I am a pharmacist with over 10 years of international experience working in healthcare management consulting. Prior to launching Handl Health I was one of the digital health leaders at Ernst & Young helping major healthcare systems and payers across the US build new digital businesses and experiences. 

I now have the privilege of leading an incredible team at Handl Health as CEO. At our core we're a healthcare price transparency platform using data machine learning and human-centered design to drive better experiences and ultimately value for enterprises and consumers.

What inspired our founding? For the first time in US history legislation has meant real prices of healthcare services are being published by the industry at scale. 

We think this is a game changer. If you're wondering why look back at how technology and legislation changed the travel industry or stock market — prices became transparent and publicly available democratizing access to information and empowering consumers to make effective decisions based on free market principles. 

The US healthcare industry is just at the beginning of this journey and the challenge today is implementing the legislation given the technical complexity and archaic systems and practices. The good news is that in this day and age we have the technology and capability to use it in a meaningful way and Handl Health is positioned to partner with the industry and help with this effort.

For us it's about leveraging the legislation to achieve better access experience and outcomes at fair market prices. Think about the way Expedia or Robinhood allowed virtually anybody to travel the world or invest in the stock market — Handl Health will do the same for healthcare breaking down financial and experience barriers to improve access and ultimately quality of life.

company_description:
Oatmeal Health is on a mission to catch lung cancer earlier especially in the communities where it's most often missed.

We built LungAI a full-stack AI diagnostic that plugs directly into the radiology software imaging centers already use. It reads CT scans faster and more accurately than the current standard of care giving radiologists/pulmonologists a nodule-level malignancy risk score in under 3 minutes. 

It's the only lung CADx with temporal modeling meaning it tracks how nodules change over time rather than just a single snapshot. And it's reimbursable under CPT 0721T generating $650 per scan in net-new revenue for imaging centers at zero upfront cost.

On the other hand we partner with FQHCs serving underserved communities where only 6% of eligible patients are ever screened. We handle patient identification navigation and clinical trial enrollment so care teams can focus on care.

Founded in 2022 by Jonathan Govette Yuri Ahuja MD PhD and Ashish Basuray PhD.

about:
Hi! I'm Josh Allan Dykstra.

I grew up in a small midwestern town raised by amazing entrepreneurial parents who taught me the future is something we design and build together.

Remember when the future felt exciting? When we dreamed of flying cars cities in the sky and a world where technology made life more delightful? That optimism got buried somewhere under decades of dystopian predictions and doomsday scenarios.

Over the last 15+ years I’ve had the honor to work with some of the best organizations on earth who collectively employ over a million people. I’ve learned how to recapture that “World Of Tomorrow” excitement — and how we can build the future we actually want to live and work in.

I’m here to share the blueprints with you.

Learn more at joshallan.com
company_description:
Hello Tomorrow is the future-focused organizational design studio founded by speaker/author/futurist Josh Allan Dykstra.

We focus on bringing clarity to chaos to help leaders design better systems that energize humanity and create a future we can all love. 

// WE ALSO HOST THE HELLO TOMORROW PODCAST // 

The Hello Tomorrow Podcast is where the future gets real.

Host Josh Allan Dykstra recovering tech founder turned optimistic futurist cuts through the noise to make sense of what's next. This isn't your typical prediction show filled with shiny tech fantasies. It's a wake-up call for change-makers leaders rebels and anyone who refuses to let the future happen TO them instead of designing it themselves.

Each episode exposes the systems that are secretly shaping tomorrow — from A.I. companies buying your creative work to why your "successful" workplace is secretly extracting your life force. Josh delivers the poignant truths about work technology and power that everyone's feeling but not quite articulating.

Because here's the thing: optimism isn't blind hope — it's a rebellion strategy. Revolutionaries are always optimists fighting for a future they want to exist. That's what this show is about.

Perfect for: Change-makers who know something's broken but can't quite name it. Leaders building the world they want to live in. Rebels who are tired of being a victim of systems designed to suck.

Not for: Billionaires people who want to be told everything's fine or anyone looking for feel-good platitudes about "disruption."

The future is counting on us to get this right. Are you ready to say hello to tomorrow?
headline: Confusion has a cost. Clarity is power. Future Of Work Keynote Speaker Sense-Maker & Business Philosopher A.I. Thought Lea

about:
My mission> Democratize access to medical imaging by removing the operational bottlenecks that slow radiology. As CEO & co-founder of Quantivly I’m building an agentic-AI “air-traffic-control” layer so every scanner runs better staff are freed to focus on care and every patient is seen sooner.

Why it matters>
Interpretive AI is easing the traditional reading backlog but idle scanners scheduling gaps and siloed workflows have become the new choke point. Quantivly automates the decisions that keep imaging moving—boosting throughput shortening wait times and letting clinicians devote more time to patients.

My path>
• Started coding at 12 youngest finalist in Prologin France’s 36-hour national programming marathon at 15
• B.Eng. Computer Science (ranked #2 2004)
• M.S. Computer Vision (2005)
• Ph.D. Applied Mathematics (2009 “Best Ph.D.” award) for *AI agent-based* brain-MRI segmentation
• Post-doc Computational Radiology Lab Boston Children’s Hospital (2009)
• AI Scientist Boston Children’s Hospital (2014)
• Assistant Professor of Radiology Harvard Medical School (2018)
• 50+ peer-reviewed papers multiple innovation awards two granted U.S. patents more pending.

The aha moment>
After 12 years in a leading pediatric radiology department I realized we measure almost everything except the operations that determine access. Quantivly is the answer: real-time situational awareness predictive orchestration and autonomous agents that coordinate rooms people and equipment.

Let’s connect>
Always up for a conversation about AI healthcare operations or scaling deep-tech startups. If you’re working to improve patient care—or just enjoy discussing agentic systems—feel free to reach out.
company_description:
Quantivly is building AI for imaging operations not clinical diagnosis.

Radiology departments operate in the same way airlines did in 1981 with no access to operational data such as jet engine performance wind statistics etc.  

Similarly imaging facilities today critically lack access to operational data to manage their multi-million-dollar MRI scanners leading to a true operational black hole and decisions based on gut feeling instead of data. 

The Quantivly platform analyzes cleans and harmonizes scanner data to build the first queryable vendor-neutral highly granular source of knowledge about operations. It is not just about what was scheduled it is also about what really happened on your scanners. 

Quantivly further leverages AI to augment the information and capture data previously unavailable including repeat detection image quality quantification and smart recommendations. Armed with data leaders can justify MR operations decisions capital purchases staffing needs workflow changes and quantify the success of their decisions and interventions with metrics. 

AI promised to revolutionize diagnosis in Radiology but adoption has been slow with minimal impact on patient care in many clinical scenarios. Quantivly instead focuses on an urgent problem of discovering operational patterns and help optimize operations increase patient access and improve patient satisfaction. 

We unlock a new world of data analytics for imaging facilities. 
And this is just the beginning. 
We have an exciting roadmap.
headline:
CEO and Co-Founder at Quantivly 
Assistant Professor at Harvard Medical School 
Senior Staff AI Scientist at Boston Children's

about:
I am a father husband a brother a son and a friend but there is one word that would describe me Courageous. In order to grow up in one of the worst neighborhoods in the city of Boston and survive when people around are constantly dropping dead you need to be courageous. In order to have a child and still complete a college education while others simply dropped out and never went back you need to be courageous. In order to start a fresh idea and have setback after setback to the point where it looks like it wouldn’t even start but now is a successful business venture you need to be courageous. 

Growing up in a loving family but in a bad neighborhood I had to overcome mountains that most people wouldn’t dream of. There was nothing handed to me. I know what it is like to come up from nothing and how hard it is to make something. It’s as if the world was predestined to be against you.

When I started Mobile Cuts I couldn’t get the right people to support my dream but I didn’t give up. When I finally got some support the laws said I couldn’t do the dream. I went back and forth with the local government until the law was changed. The people who were with me on launch night most of them are missing today. With every opposition I had to be courageous. 

The world tried to stop me from birth. Life tried to stop my progress. The law tried to stop my dreams but they all underestimated the courage and tenacity of Montrez Williams. 

The C.E.O. of Mobile Cuts the father of a college graduate the husband of an amazing wife a friend and inspiration to many. The man who redefined the word Courageous.

about:
Nutrition is often overlooked in places where it should be considered crucial. Like hospitals!

HealthLeap is an AI 'safety net' that helps prevent hospital patients with malnutrition (affects 30%+) from falling through the cracks and help them get treatment much earlier.

As a clinical dietitian I wanted to do much more to help patients who were missing out on nutrition care they needed so I started hacking together software to help myself and colleagues treat patients faster.
