about:
Scientist in Genomics elucidating causalities underlying phenotype formation in health and disease. Passionate technologist with expertise in single-cell and spatial sequencing technologies as well as genome-wide genomics profiling approaches. Activities in translational and clinical science with a strong focus on immune-based diagnostic and therapeutic strategies. 

My Single Cell Genomics group at the Spanish National Center for Genomic Analysis (CNAG) joins biotechnology with computational biology to establish best practices in single-cell and spatial research. The group combines collaborative work and Research & Development activities with a focus on Immuno-oncology (IO) and Immune-mediated inflammatory diseases (IMIDs). I am member of the Human Cell Atlas (HCA) project with projects to generate atlases of human immune cells the kidney and the pancreas. I am co-chairing the Spatial Taskforce the Industry Partnership Program (IPP) and the Standards and Technology working group (STWG) to scout benchmark and scale emerging technologies for the HCA and Genomics community. 

As Scientific Co-founder and Chief Scientific Officer of Omniscope I bring high-resolution profiling technologies to clinical application developing advanced diagnostics and therapeutics tools. Omniscope is a Systems Immunology company providing solutions to detect diseases for interceptive medicine and to develop next-generation cell therapies. Reading the immune system as universal sensor of diseases we envision Omniscope’s technologies to enable modern medicine and to disrupt current practices in health care.

company_description:
Compassion 2.0 was created from the belief that the next competitive advantage isn’t just technological—it’s relationship grounded in care. In a world dominated by extraction and speed we know that resonance performance and measurement are not “soft ideas.” They are the foundation of sustainable success and can be measured with the same rigor as revenue.

Our mission is to embed compassionate leadership human flourishing and psychological safety into the very architecture of organizations systems products and services. Grounded in neuroscience organizational design and the evidence-based ROI of Care framework we empower leaders to create cultures where trust fuels creativity collaboration and growth.

One of our core vehicles for this work is the Chief Flourishing Officer (CFLO) Learning Journey & Mastermind—a multi-pod cohort-based program launching on Sept 8 Oct 13 and Nov 10. This experience is designed for executives HR leaders and culture architects to explore test and embed the ROI of Care in their organizations. Through shared learning applied experimentation and cross-industry exchange participants cultivate leadership strategies that elevate both people and performance.

Complementing this our platform Flourishingly provides science-informed diagnostics dashboards and a curated Enterprise Stack of interventions to support flourishing at individual team and organizational levels. It helps leaders not only measure the depth of the relational field in their organizations but also apply targeted practices to strengthen it—transforming data into meaningful people-centered action.

We are calling on leaders who know that care is not a perk but a strategic imperative. Together we can design a future where organizations flourish because people do.

about:
My superpower is taking fuzzy ideas and making them into usable consumable things. 

Those ‘things’ have included works of fiction a patented digital storytelling platform a healthcare SaaS platform addiction recovery programming in a Mississippi prison a civic imagination initiative and dozen brands cultures and languages that breed perfect pitch decks to thriving companies. To name a few.

As a founding partner of InnoEngine an innovation strategy and execution group we help good ideas come to life by using human-centered design to build intuitive intelligent tools products projects and companies. This builds on the models from my original consultancy Lunae Innovation Lab where we used to say was to go from "idea to thing " and that my job was to work myself out of a job. Get it up going thriving — and then hand it off to the amazing teams that operate. InnoEngine is that at scale.

As a Launch Lab InnoEngine has been the zero-to-one design force behind Oria Health Kith & Kin's KinKeeper App Origin Story Health the Metals Innovation Initiative BG 2050 the Google product launch of Jigsaw's Sensemaker Tool Suite through the "What Could BG Be?" project. 

My core passion is in the transformation of healthcare humans and the communities within which we operate. Origin Story Health is value-based and data-driven care delivery that seeks to help therapy modalities and behavioral interventions to go from “healthcare” to “healing care.” BG2050 and WCBGB are getting entire towns to work together to imagine and _build_ the future of our community.

I’m an advocate for patient civil rights a data brain on health data compliance a nerd on interoperability and I am deeply committed to thinking through the hard stuff up to solve third-wave tech problems in the public and private sector. After all no one’s coming to do it for us. And if nothing changes? Well nothing changes.

Things that are easy to use are hard to build things that are easy to build end up being hard to use. My job is to help organizations get “simple” right. Through thoughtful data architecture simpler UI and persona-driven problem solving my teams help any organization make fuzzy clear and ideas into things. 

From problem statement to product marketing from imagination to iteration the InnoEngine team believes in bringing good experiences to every industry and bringing healthy cultures to the teams that power the world around us.
company_description:
Founded by Sam Ford Amanda Havard and Vijay Kamineni InnoEngine is an innovation process strategy firm that helps organizations across sectors unlock new value often by doing something they’ve never done before or through a model they haven’t used before.

Our team believes that innovation is a repeatable model. It may be shepherding Fortune 100 companies through ambitious projects. It may be supporting pre-revenue startups go into market. It may be helping multi-stakeholder initiatives form and sustain. 

Whatever the client and sector InnoEngine’s models help teams get going projects get unstuck and communities and organizations operating like efficient effective engines of innovation.

InnoEngine leverages our shared expertise and our model for the Six Gears of the Innovation Engine to support evolution and solutions for communities and organizations—and to help initiatives find traction when they are idling. 

We are committed to solving practical problems with practical innovation through net new solutions and multi-stakeholder partnerships. And we believe that a fixed mindset fixes nothing new while an innovation mindset makes room for imagination. An innovation mindset not only looks at what is but is willing to ask what *could* be.

Our team of InnoEngineers bring vast experience leading innovation initiatives across a broad range of sectors--manufacturing healthcare media & entertainment government higher education technology retail and more. InnoEngine partners with the organizations and communities they support from idea to impact. And we iterate on those impactful projects by helping our partners create Repeatable Patterns and Models (RPM).

InnoEngine itself was taken from idea to impact in an old mall that has been adaptively reused as infrastructure for an innovation ecosystem: the Western Kentucky University Innovation Campus just north of Nashville Tennessee in Kentucky’s fastest growing city–Bowling Green KY.

company_description: Bolden Therapeutics is a biotechnology company developing first-in-class therapeutics to treat central nervous system diseases such as Alzheimer's and ischemic stroke. The company's scientific founders have identified a key molecular pathway to stimulate neural stem cells to promote the birth of new neurons (neurogenesis) in the adult brain. In a proprietary mouse model Bolden has established a proof-of-concept of this approach to increase neurogenesis and enhance hippocampal-dependent memory. The company is actively developing antisense oligonucleotide and monoclonal antibody therapeutics to modulate this pathway.

Tim has a proven track-record in developing and translating technologies for medical devices. He specializes in bioengineering technology with an emphasis on sensors interface electronics and control algorithms.

He was named a technical fellow in 2010 received Medtronic's highest technical and scientific award membership in the Bakken Society in 2012 and the Wallin Leadership award in 2014. Tim was recently named to the College of Fellows for the American Institute of Medical and Biological Engineering. Prior to joining Medtronic he worked as a Senior Design Engineer with the Micromachined Products Division at Analog Devices.

Outside of work he is a frequent academic guest lecturer on neural engineering circuit designs and applications. He also served as an associate editor of the IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Circuits and Systems an associate editor of the IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering and sits on the editorial board of the Journal of Neural Engineering. Tim served on the program committee for the IEEE International Solid-State Circuits Conference (Imagers MEMS Medical and Displays) from 2008-2011.

Tim Denison received his S.M. and Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and his A.B. in Physics from the University of Chicago. After a hiatus he returned to school to finish the Executive MBA program at Booth the University of Chicago where he was named a Wallman Scholar.

company_description:
Willamette the first university in the West is an innovative leader powered by rigorous education groundbreaking scholarship and original thinking. Willamette provides a learning environment where subjects converge and collide teaching students to generate new ideas across industries and preparing them for the careers of the future.

Based in Salem Oregon Willamette encourages students to pursue diverse interests through an array of experiential learning opportunities. Willamette offers study abroad programs in more than 45 countries and awards more than 75 undergraduate research grants annually. Whether our students are designing cancer-fighting molecules on campus or analyzing ecosystems in our Zena research forest they're creating knowledge that enhances our understanding of the world.

Willamette's location is unmatched. The state Capitol and numerous state agencies are right across the street giving students opportunities to spearhead legislation intern with the Governor's Office and work with the state's top scientists and economists. Students can also gain professional experience through internships in the greater Willamette Valley.

Our commitment to civic engagement is embodied in the university motto "Not unto ourselves alone are we born." The Willamette community contributes more than 70 000 service hours annually helping people throughout Salem and partnering with organizations across the globe. 

Willamette offers more than 50 undergraduate programs in the College of Arts & Sciences. The university is also home to the College of Law the Atkinson Graduate School of Management and the Pacific Northwest College of Art giving undergraduate and graduate students access to unique degree paths and unparalleled learning experiences.

Posts on Willamette University's social media are governed by the Social Media Guidelines and university conduct policies. For more information go to willamette.edu/offices/comm/social-media/guidelines/index.html.

Verras’ solutions and recent changes in the healthcare market have now uniquely positioned Verras to meet a new challenge. As a result of regulatory and reimbursement changes the Verras offerings are addressing the unique needs of the Medicare population in the United States.

Verras has introduced its suite of solutions into a singular approach for Medicare recipients. This approach “Verras’ Medicare Choices™” uses the power of its analytical tool kit. It allows seniors and their families to choose the hospital to provide the very best care within the US. about:
As the Co-Founder and CEO of Novobeing I’m committed to revolutionizing the hospital experience through enterprise XR solutions that enhance patient well-being and outcomes. Our mission is to transform in-patient care by integrating state-of-the-art VR and AR technology aiming to make medical XR headsets an essential part of every hospital room. At Novobeing envision a future where these tools improve patient satisfaction and accelerate recovery for millions of patients worldwide.

Our journey began in a hospital room where I supported my father during his battle with cancer. Witnessing the stress and anxiety he endured inspired me to create a technological solution to alleviate emotional burdens and enhance patient care. Recognizing the potential of VR/AR I assembled a team of experts from Harvard MGH Hollywood Gaming and the digital therapetuics space to develop the Novobeing platform. 

Today Novobeing's platform is deployed in leading health systems enhancing patient experiences and driving faster recoveries. Our solutions not only improve quality of life but also offer cost savings for healthcare providers. With a foundation built on rigorous science and proven treatment protocols we’re making a tangible impact in healthcare.

Today's healthcare system faces unprecedented challenges including a growing elderly population and a projected shortage of healthcare workers. Up to 60% of in-patients experience high distress worsening symptoms and escalating costs. Our platform addresses these issues by reducing distress improving care quality and mitigating financial strain on hospitals.

I’m passionate about leveraging technology to create a more efficient and compassionate healthcare system committed to expanding our impact and refining our solutions for a better future.

Strengths: Product Vision Strategy Business Development Sales Leadership

about:
David Mills is a Distinguished Professor in the Department of Food Science & Technology at the University of California at Davis. Dr. Mills studies the molecular biology and ecology of bacteria that play an active role in gut health. During the last 26 years Dr. Mills has mentored over 32 graduate students and postdocs generating over 225 publications including seminal work on lactic acid bacterial and bifidobacterial genomics. In the early 2000s Dr. Mills helped create the UC Davis “Milk Group” a consortium of researchers who investigate and translate the uniquely beneficial aspects of human and bovine milk. Professor Mills was named the Peter J. Shields Chair in Dairy Food Science in 2012 in 2015 he was elected a Fellow in the American Academy of Microbiology and in 2023 he was named a UC Davis Distinguished Professor. Since 2018 he has been designated a Highly Cited Researcher by Clarivate Analytics. Dr. Mills’s research has helped launch several startup companies.

Specialties: Bacterial genetics/genomics food microbiology fermentation microbiology intestinal microbiology probiotics/prebiotics gut health milk bioactives

about:
I started REPROSENT to solve one of the most overlooked gaps in oncology: we’ve modeled tumors drugs and populations - but we still don’t measure how patients actually tolerate treatment day to day.

Our IRB-cleared platform captures real-time symptom data to support dose optimization adverse event prediction and safety adaptation in trials and care. We built it to enable FDA-aligned trial design support safer more personalized dosing and power real-world AI.

This missing layer - the lived human signal - is essential to making precision medicine truly work.

REPROSENT is backed by Y Combinator and winner of the Astellas Pharma Changing Cancer Care Award.

Before this I spent 20+ years building products across medtech digital health and AI - leading launches at Intel and Ciitizen (Invitae) and advising startups at the intersection of health and data.
current_position_description:
REPROSENT decodes real-time patient symptom data into actionable signal - powering safer trials better dosing and adaptive care starting in cancer.

Our IRB-cleared platform enables early detection of disease progression predicts adverse events and creates a continuous feedback loop grounded in daily patient experience - unlocking the human input precision medicine has been missing.

REPROSENT is backed by Y Combinator Harvard Hustle Fund and Bond.
Astellas Oncology 2022 C3 Grand Prize Winner
WomensNet Distinguished Business Award
company_description:
Oncology has sequenced genomes built AI on claims data and mapped tumor biology in extraordinary detail. But no one has captured how patients actually experience therapy between visits.

REPROSENT embeds directly in clinical programs to capture that signal in real time. Every cohort builds a proprietary dataset that cannot be reconstructed retroactively - and compounds with every program added.

ADCs oral KRAS inhibitors combination regimens - all hitting the same tolerability wall. The infrastructure to understand it is being built now.

Backed by Y Combinator Harvard Hustle Fund and Bond. Astellas Oncology C3 Grand Prize Winner.

about:
I'm passionate about changing the way we cure disease. Current methods of drug discovery manufacture and delivery are not meeting our needs as a modern society. To improve how we cure disease I co-founded Parallel Bio to design and engineer better tools that are inspired by human biology. Our first innovation is a human 'immune system in a dish' to discover drugs and immunotherapies more likely to work in patients. Our platform has all of the same elements as a human immune system meaning you can test drugs and vaccines as if you were testing them in actual patients from the start. The biggest reason why 92% of new drugs fail is that they were tested in mice - but with our human platform pharma companies will know which drugs will successfully treat patients speeding up the process and reducing the cost. This product will be the first step in dramatically re-imagining tools to treat and cure the toughest diseases we face today and tomorrow.

When I’m not working on Parallel Bio you can find me learning German attending the symphony or an art exhibit and with my nose in a good book. I also enjoy travel and will try any kind of food once.

about: Tomás is passionate about developing assistive technologies but more generally he wants to design the human of the future (he wants to be a Cyborg!) - to create new and extend existing human sensory motor and cognitive abilities in order to enhance human performance beyond natural baseline. For this he is developing new input and output interfaces to increase the bandwidth between humans and computers. Using these augmentative devices he hopes to learn how the brain structurally and functionally reorganizes to accommodate new synthetic senses control capabilities and cognitive prostheses. He is interested in how neural plasticity can be exploited how humans learn and encode information and how perception can be affected (and possibly engineered!) at a conscious and subconscious level. He holds a double-major in Computer Science and Cognitive Science from UC Berkeley and a Master's in Media Arts and Sciences from the MIT Media Lab.

about:
Samantha Citro Alexander is the Co-Founder and CEO of the FoodHealth Company home of the FoodHealth Score—a data-driven nutrition scoring system designed to make food health transparent measurable and actionable.

A former beauty exec turned food health advocate Sam spent a decade at brands like Clinique and Smashbox where she helped legacy brands transform to meet the clean beauty movement. Now she's bringing that same clarity and accountability to food. Her mission: make it easy for people and institutions to understand what's actually in our food—and how it affects our health.

Frustrated by misleading labels and wellness marketing Sam brought a team together to create the FoodHealth Score: a 1–100 rating that demystifies food health using real nutrition science not fads or hype. It's now used by retailers food brands and data providers to drive smarter decisions at every level of the food system.

Under her leadership FoodHealth Company has partnered with industry giants like NielsenIQ Kroger and Hy-Vee to embed health scoring into shopping data retail experiences and food benefit programs. Together they're making it possible to measure the nutritional quality of everything in your cart—and compare trends across demographics regions and payment types.

Sam has been featured in Forbes and Inc and is a frequent speaker at HLTH Food-as-Medicine Summits and national policy forums where she challenges brands and institutions to move from spin to substance—and make food health as transparent as calorie counts.

Her belief? The future of health is food —and we all deserve to know what we're really eating.

about:
Laura Ash is a wellness business creative Clinical Herbalist feminist mother and social entrepreneur dedicated to preserving cultural knowledge and biodiversity. With over 22 years in the herbal medicine industry Laura has helped build companies with brands that are relevant that foster meaningful cross-cultural relationships. Her work bridges traditional healing arts and modern business strategies guided by her commitment to equity sustainability art and the earth as a primary stakeholder.

Laura became a Clinical Herbalist in 2005 studying under medical herbalists at the California School of Herbal Studies and Ayurveda at the Ayurvedic Institute and many folk and Indigenous herbalists. Her time living in the UK and East Africa ignited her passion for health sovereignty and cultural preservation. In 2011 she founded the Herbal Anthropology Project a nonprofit dedicated to protecting Traditional Knowledge including the Maasai Traditional Medicine practices in Tanzania through frameworks like the Traditional Knowledge Division of the World Intellectual Property Organization.

Laura was the owner of the legacy business The Scarlet Sage Herb Co. in San Francisco a retail intersection of commerce and access to healing plant medicine products and education. To further amplify the voices of healers and herbalists Laura founded Land of Verse. This online wellness education platform serves as a "Masterclass for healers " bringing trusted experts in the healing arts to a global audience from beginners to advanced practitioners. Laura has a love for wellness branding that speaks to the naysayers making the trusted voices relevant to a larger audience.

Currently Laura is focused on building BPRA to support biodiversity conservation Traditional Knowledge preservation and develop equity in plant research. 

Her professional expertise includes manifesting businesses from ideation to creation branding and creatives operations strategic growth design thinking and fostering equitable partnerships across cultures.

about:
Richard Shane Ph.D. is an internationally-renowned Sleep Expert and the Founder and Chief Clinical Officer of Sleep Easy LLC Sleep Easy is a clinically-proven sleep solution that treats even the most challenging sleep issues. It does not involve sleep restriction meditation hypnosis visualization or deep breathing. It is a technique that goes far beyond just relaxation. 

Sleep Easy guides you through calming body sensations that are neurological switches that calm your mind emotions and body easing towards sleep. This enables you to fall asleep or back to sleep more quickly easily and deeply...even in the midst of stress and unusual hours. People report Sleep Easy is far more effective than any other method they have used with 81.6% showing better sleep in the first few nights in clinical trials.

Since 2009 Dr. Shane has been the Behavioral Sleep Therapist for one of the largest primary care practices in the nation which serves over 200 000 patients.

2009-2014 he was the Behavioral Sleep Therapist for Exempla-Lutheran Medical Center Sleep Center Denver. 

His Sleep Easy Method has been used by major corporations including Hewlett-Packard Ball Aerospace Coors Brewing Company Public Service Marathon Oil CH2M Hill Micromotion and Quantum.

Dr. Richard Shane has been featured in over 100 medical journals and publications including Psychology Today Men’s Journal Shape Dr. Oz the Good Life Huffington Post Reader’s Digest International Journal for Sleeping Disorders among many others. Dr. Shane has worked with large-scale organizations including the U.S. Military personnel the oil and gas industry commercial airline police and fire department municipalities and many more. Dr. Shane is on a mission to treat millions of people worldwide who suffer from severe insomnia and other sleep disorders - and transform the world one night at a time.

company_description:
We provide safe effective ketamine therapy for people seeking relief from depression

We founded Ember Health because we were frustrated by the lack of effective medical treatment options for the millions of people struggling with severe depression. We know we share this feeling with the thousands of compassionate mental healthcare professionals who provide critical counsel to their patients. They desire better more reliable complements to their care. 

We believe ketamine is a powerful answer to this need: Whereas fewer than 30% of patients on traditional antidepressants realize relief of their depressive symptoms low-dose infusions of ketamine have shown an average of 75% improvement in depression. While oral antidepressant medication can take six to eight weeks to take effect and have unwanted side effects ketamine takes effect almost immediately after administration with minimal if any harmful effect.

Join as at https://emberhealth.co/ to learn more.

company_description:
SimpliFed is democratizing access to baby feeding and breastfeeding services by providing high-quality virtual care that is covered by insurance. The Ithaca New York-based company envisions a world where every parent feels good about feeding their family – because baby feeding and breastfeeding is central to maternal and infant health. Founded in 2019 by Andrea Ippolito a biomedical engineer and mom of two SimpliFed provides evidence-based and inclusive virtual baby feeding services to everyone no matter how they choose to feed their baby. Simplifed's mission is to create a new model for optimal baby feeding by building trusted relationships with parents partnering with healthcare payers and providers and shifting policy practice and narratives to drive health and economic equity starting with baby's first foods.
For more information on SimpliFed: https://www.simplifed.com/

about:
As Founder of The Ignite Writers Collective Ruthie offers a variety of services to support the writing process including book proposal and manuscript development and writing workshops for those who want to tell their own stories. Ruthie's clients are USA Today Bestsellers have received major non-fiction book deals with Big 5 publishers and have gotten agent representation by Aevitas Creative Management Park & Fine Literary and Media and Janklow & Nesbit. Her clients’ essays have been published in The New York Times Electric Literature HuffPost Personals Vogue Cosmo Catapult Ms. Today.com Tablet Health Magazine New England Journal of Medicine Well + Good and more.

Prior to founding The Ignite Writers Collective Ruthie was the Deputy Editor at ForbesWomen. Throughout her career she has profiled game-changing entrepreneurs and social activists and written extensively about the professional and financial advancement of women. She has been awarded journalism fellowships to war-torn Liberia and Lebanon and her writing has also been published in Vogue Glamour O Magazine The New York Times The Atlantic The Wall Street Journal Forbes Salon Slate Newsweek and more. But the piece she’s most proud of is her New York Times Modern Love essay about her own failed egg freezing. “Don’t Put All Your (Frozen) Eggs in One Basket” became the launching point for her upcoming memoir and shifted her focus from traditional reporting to personal stories. 

Her memoir The Mother Code was published in May 2025 by Random House. Synthesizing reportage and memoir The Mother Code unravels how we’ve come to understand the institution of motherhood. What emerges is a groundbreaking new vision for what it means to parent: a mother code that goes beyond our bloodlines and genetics and instead urges us to embrace inheritance as the legacy we want to leave behind for those we love.

“A warm candid memoir with a clear-eyed take on the conflicting pressures placed on Gen-X women and all the ways choosing one path in life means forsaking others. The Mother Code will soothe the anxious and help women at a crossroads make peace with the unknown.” — Ada Calhoun NYT bestselling author of Why We Can’t Sleep 

Ruthie currently lives in Brooklyn with her family.

company_description:
Kythera Labs provides a data management and analytics platform to process healthcare data of all types and apply machine learning to look for signals that can report and predict behavioral patterns of patients practitioners health systems and payers.

We help healthcare information companies build enterprise-scale products to serve their customers and we help all types of healthcare organizations (e.g. hospitals pharmaceutical manufacturers technology companies government entities) understand the performance of their markets and the individual entities within them.

Our sample of over 300 million US patient-lives served from our cloud-based machine learning-driven platform brings scale and speed that performs exponentially faster than any other technology in the healthcare data and analytics world. Bring your own data to our platform and we will combine it with ours to give you an unsurpassed view of healthcare events at a level of fidelity and quality that is quickly actionable and highly affordable.

The team at Kythera Labs has been solving problems for decades using proven data science concepts and unique technologies to enable decision making and growth for healthcare organizations. Our focus has been to be humble and curious in all of our work. We have learned life is too short to not enjoy the journey. Our team understands that trust is at the heart of innovation and it is something we work at everyday.

company_description:
Britebound™ (formerly American Student Assistance®) helps young people navigate one of the most important questions they'll ever face: What comes next?

We inspire learners to explore their passions experiment with potential careers and confidently chart a path toward their future whether or not that path includes a traditional college degree. At the center of our work is a simple but powerful message we share with every student we reach: Find your why. Find your way.

We believe every young person deserves the opportunity to discover the intersection between what they love what they're good at what the world needs and what they can be paid for. Yet too many students—especially those facing systemic barriers—lack access to the tools experiences and guidance needed to make informed decisions about their future.

That's where Britebound comes in.

Through free digital tools philanthropy research impact investing and advocacy we work alongside students educators families employers mission-aligned nonprofits and businesses and community partners to expand access to career readiness resources and economic opportunity. 

Our approach bridges education and workforce systems helping students move from curiosity to clarity and from possibility to purpose.
By aligning passion with preparation and opportunity we aim to ensure that every young person is equipped not just to choose a path but to build a meaningful and sustainable future.
headline: People Focused Technology

FamTech Collaborative Company
Techstars Portfolio Company
gener8tor Portfolio Company

OndeCare reinvents in-home dependent care providing families a flexible solution to their caregiving needs and businesses a timely employee benefit while generating worthwhile jobs to local care safety and education professionals.

OndeCare rebalances the home care marketplace for all parties. Our mobile app systematically books the right caregiver for the right family at the right price for both. Our proprietary system also ensures care fulfillment with automated back-up.

OndeCare is a matching and bidding system unlike any solution out there - making it easy and quick to secure quality care. We don’t stop there though OndeCare elevates service reliability too through workflow notifications and in-app job reporting and payment. Finally OndeCare is eligible for Dependent Care Flexible Spending Account (DCFSA) and Long Term Care Insurance reimbursements and eliminates Household Employee tax responsibilities for clients.

• For businesses OndeCare is the customizable pay-as-you-go backup care employee benefit that sustains productivity stems employee turnover and saves money. 

• For home care agencies OndeCare is the essential workforce backup to help maintain clients and more smoothly onboard new clients as demand grows. 

• For today’s busy families OndeCare is the safest easiest and most dependable source of pre-vetted in-home caregivers for all loved ones & circumstances. 

• For caregivers OndeCare is the way to add to your income on your own terms - choose your rate choose when you want to work and work for one (1) family at a time. 

If you are a retired retiring actively working or studying to be a nurse teacher nurse's aide EMT Direct Support Professional Firefighter etc - go to OndeCare.com to Get Started.

about:
I Co-Founded and serve as Chief Medical Officer for Tend-Health and it's animal health subsidiary - Amend Pet. We are transforming the microbiome health industry through providing access to microbiome restoration and diversity analytics. We have assembled a team of experts in the field of microbiome sciences engineering data analytics and medical device commercialization to provide a scalable solution for animal and human gut sufferers.

As a Pediatric Urologist at Seattle Children’s Hospital and Professor in the Department of Urology at the University of Washington my clinical and research has focused on improving surgical skills education training and development. My work has been published in the domains of surgical technology medical device design and development of scalable technologies to enhance healthcare. I am an Entrepreneur Surgeon.

I recently co-founded Singletto which develops and licenses technology using light-activated dyes to create novel pathogen-destroying singlet oxygen solutions. We work with the foremost experts and entrepreneurial leaders in pathogen-destroying technology. As Lead Author on the Development of Methods for Mask and N95 Decontamination (DeMaND) study in collaboration with the World Health Organization the CDC and university and laboratory partners around the world we found light-activated dyes to provide 99.99% inactivation of the SARS-CoV-2 virus. This study and other research will guide singlet-oxygen solutions to the foreground of the coronavirus fight and the fight against all pathogens worldwide.

In later phases the platform will sport features pertinent to drug/dose recommendations further alleviating the “trial-and-error” problem pervasive in epilepsy management.

Our long-term vision includes expanding Neuroliten to support other neurological & behavioral conditions as well.

company_description:
InPlanet is a leading ClimateTech start-up pioneering Enhanced Rock Weathering (ERW) as a measurable scalable and verified carbon removal solution. As the first company in the world to independently certify ERW carbon credits InPlanet ensures high-quality permanent CO₂ sequestration while regenerating tropical soils. Backed by Stripe Shopify and top climate investors InPlanet helps companies the world over achieve net zero by removing CO₂ at gigaton scale.

 Empowering Farmers Restoring Ecosystems By applying finely ground silicate rock to tropical farmlands farmers capture and store CO₂ while improving soil health boosting crop yields and reducing reliance on chemical fertilizers. This regenerative approach enhances soil fertility water retention and nutrient availability making farms more resilient to climate change. InPlanet supports farmers ensuring they benefit economically and environmentally while driving global carbon removal efforts.

 A Science-Backed Scalable Solution Enhanced Rock Weathering is a natural process where silicate rocks react with CO₂ forming stable bicarbonates that store carbon for millennia. By applying rock powders to farmland InPlanet accelerates this process delivering verified carbon removal healthier soils and more sustainable agriculture.

 Built by Climate Experts Driven by Impact Founded by Felix Harteneck (CEO) and Niklas Kluger (COO) InPlanet is powered by a multidisciplinary team of scientists engineers and climate experts committed to scaling nature-based solutions.

 Join the Movement As demand for permanent carbon removal grows InPlanet is making nature-based CDR a reality. We collaborate with farmers scientists policymakers and businesses to build a sustainable future where climate action and agriculture go hand in hand.

 For partnerships carbon credits or career opportunities visit https://inplanet.earth/ and sign up for our newsletter for the latest updates!

I'm endlessly curious about the human experience and obsessed with one question: what does it mean to be fully human in an age of digital overload?
 
In a world of fractured attention and AI-driven overwhelm I remind organizations and leaders of their real competitive edge... their humanity.
 
The research is clear: our connection to technology is inversely correlated to effectiveness and productivity.
I help leaders and teams rebalance that relationship so they can exceed their goals and step into their highest potential.

current_position_2_description:
Mark Groves Consulting exists at the intersection of humanity and technology. Built on the foundation of Create the Love: a relational education platform with over 26 million downloads over 30 000 students and more than 1.2 million followers worldwide —> we bring that same depth of human understanding into the boardroom.

Here's what the science actually says: more connectivity doesn't equal more productivity. It equals more distractions. And in those distractions teams lose focus leaders lose presence and organizations lose their most valuable asset... genuine human connection.
We don't teach people to quit their phones. We teach them something harder and more powerful: how to be in a conscious relationship with technology so it serves their life instead of running it.

The future isn't analog. It isn't digital. It's human.

Through keynotes workshops and leadership consulting we help organizations and their people optimize their relationship to technology — so they can lead with clarity connect with depth and build the kind of teams and lives that actually feel worth showing up for.

The competitive edge of the future isn't a better app. It's you.

Harsha K. Rajasimha Ph.D. is a Virginia Tech alumnus entrepreneur and healthcare innovator who is transforming how clinical trials are designed managed and delivered worldwide. As Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Jeeva Clinical Trials he has pioneered one of the industry's first AI-ready unified clinical research platforms helping biopharmaceutical companies academic medical centers research sites and patients collaborate through a single digital ecosystem. Under his leadership the platform supports dozens of clinical studies and tens of thousands of users globally advancing faster more efficient and patient-centric clinical research.
 
A former scientist trained at the National Institutes of Health and U.S. Food and Drug Administration Dr. Rajasimha combines expertise in biomedical engineering data science artificial intelligence and regulatory science to modernize the infrastructure underpinning clinical development. His work focuses on replacing fragmented legacy systems with scalable digital platforms that accelerate therapeutic innovation while improving data quality compliance and patient engagement.
 
Beyond entrepreneurship Dr. Rajasimha is a nationally recognized rare disease advocate and the Founder and Chairperson of Indo US Organization for Rare Diseases a nonprofit dedicated to advancing rare disease research global collaboration and patient-centered innovation. As a rare disease parent himself he has spent more than a decade building bridges between patients researchers industry and policymakers to expand access to treatments and clinical research opportunities.
 
Embodying Virginia Tech's spirit of Ut Prosim ("That I May Serve") Dr. Rajasimha has dedicated his career to leveraging technology science and entrepreneurship to improve human health and accelerate the development of life-changing therapies for patients worldwide. His accomplishments reflect the impact Virginia Tech alumni are making at the intersection of engineering innovation and service.

about:
I’m Dr. Nishit Pancholi Co-Founder Chief Operating Officer and Chief Medical Officer of Jointechlabs a USA-based regenerative medicine company pioneering point-of-care solutions to help people heal naturally without labs costly infrastructure or prolonged downtime. With 15+ years of experience in stem cell science and business operations I focus on bringing regenerative medicine from bench to bedside in the most accessible and efficient way possible.

I lead the organization through clinical strategy IP development regulatory submissions product validation and international market entry. I’ve helped raise capital build strategic partnerships and shape the company’s scientific and commercial roadmap alongside top KOLs and academic partners.

 A Vision for the Future of Point-of-Care Regenerative Medicine 
We developed MiniTC® (FDA-cleared) and Mini-Stem System® (CE-Mark approved in the EU) to make regenerative therapies accessible at the clinic level—no lab no capital infrastructure just fast in-clinic results. These devices isolate stem-cell-rich microfat and SVF from a patient’s own fat tissue at the point-of-care in.
We’re not just commercializing devices—we’re building the foundation for a global regenerative medicine standard. Our goal is to empower clinicians with accessible science-backed tools to reduce pain restore mobility and enhance quality of life—naturally.

 Our core technologies:
 MiniTC®: Portable disposable FDA-cleared medical device for isolating regenerative microfat at the point of care. Total processing time around 30 minutes in any clinical setting.
 Mini-Stem System®: CE-Mark approved expands capabilities to isolate Stromal Vascular Fraction (SVF) for broader regenerative uses
 JTL-T-01: Injectable stem cell scaffold for osteoarthritis NIH-funded and under FDA pre-IDE pathway
 JTL-T-02: Microfat-based 3D printed collagen scaffolds for chronic wounds

 Market Traction and Growth
 FDA 510(k) clearance CE Mark ISO13485 and MDSAP certifications
 14+ granted patents
 Early commercial sales and revenue
 Distribution partnerships in the U.S. EU and Asia
 Pipeline therapies aimed at FDA fast-track approval
 Expanding access in global markets: China India Vietnam UAE Brazil

 Inquire about investing or partnering with Jointechlabs. I look forward to connecting with you.
current_position: Co-Founder Chief Operating Officer and Chief Medical Officer
current_position_description:
As Co-founder and Chief Operating Officer I’ve led the development of FDA-cleared portable devices that bring advanced regenerative medicine directly to clinics eliminating the need for costly labs or complex infrastructure. Our technology isolates stem cell-rich microfat and stromal vascular fraction from a patient’s own fat tissue at the point-of-care enabling rapid effective treatments for conditions like osteoarthritis soft tissue injuries aesthetic applications and chronic wounds. 

We’ve secured regulatory approvals in the U.S. and EU generated early revenue and expanded distribution into key international markets including China and India. Beyond devices we’re advancing first-in-class biologic therapies with NIH support to tackle unmet medical needs. 

My vision is to make regenerative therapies accessible worldwide improve patient outcomes and build scalable commercial opportunities. We’re actively seeking strategic partners to accelerate market adoption and drive our therapeutic pipeline forward.

 Market Opportunity & Impact
The regenerative medicine market is projected to exceed $160 billion globally by 2030. Jointechlabs targets critical unmet needs in orthopedics chronic wound care and aesthetic medicine delivering scalable solutions that enhance patient quality of life and reduce healthcare costs.

 Competitive Advantage
Our patented platform uniquely combines portability disposability and speed delivering stem cell therapies at the point of care without reliance on centralized labs. This streamlined approach and regulatory clearances position us ahead of competitors relying on slower more complex technologies.

 Team & Leadership Strength
With over 15 years in regenerative medicine stem cell research and medical device commercialization I lead a team of seasoned physician-entrepreneurs and scientists. Supported by top clinical regulatory and business advisors we are well-positioned to drive innovation and global growth.

Most specialty care practices lose revenue they already earned. It leaks out through denied claims underbilling and write-offs that never had to happen. about:
Desh Mohan MD is a practicing internal medicine physician and the Co-Founder and Chief Medical Officer at Koda Health a digital health company building AI-powered infrastructure to support serious illness care planning at scale.

Koda’s platform helps individuals document and manage their care preferences—from legal advance directives to condition-specific planning like Kidney Action Plans—while integrating directly into provider and payer workflows. Through its flagship product in Advance Care Planning (ACP) Koda ensures that every care decision reflects what matters most to the patient.

Desh leads Koda’s clinical strategy bringing together his background in medicine and digital health to ensure the platform fits seamlessly into real-world care. He co-founded the company during his time as a Digital Health Biodesign Fellow at the Texas Medical Center Innovation Institute where he and his co-founders identified one of the most overlooked challenges in healthcare: the lack of accessible effective tools to support advance care planning before a crisis occurs.

He earned his B.S. from MIT his M.D. from UT Southwestern and completed his Internal Medicine residency at Baylor College of Medicine. Along the way he also worked on healthcare infrastructure projects in Tanzania Haiti and India—formative experiences that continue to shape his commitment to building systems rooted in dignity empathy and access.

Today Koda Health supports over 1 million patients through partnerships with organizations like Cigna Privia and Houston Methodist. The company has raised $6 million in seed funding backed by strategic investors including AARP and Memorial Hermann.

To learn more visit kodahealth.com.

about:
After several years as a practicing ER physician I went back to school and earned a Ph.D. in Clinical Psychology because I wanted to better understand the psychological and emotional impact of traumatic injury. My dissertation work focused on the role of dissociation during trauma (what we now call the freeze response) in the integration of the experience. In the years since I've continued to study teach and write about stress and trauma.

Teaching in a school of Public Health and for the Center for Mind-Body Medicine doing community level trauma resilience training has broadened my understanding of stress and trauma beyond the individual to communities and populations. Every year I'm honored to meet and work with a new cohort of public health graduate students who never fail to renew my faith in the potential of human intelligence dedication and kindness.

The newest chapter in my career is my role as Chief Medical Officer at Neolth since 2018. This job calls on my experiences in higher education medicine survey research and mental health in an exciting and innovative way. As a professor I have long been concerned with the mental well being of my students and sought ways to minimize academic stress for them as best I can. It's very exciting to be part of something that goes beyond mitigating damage to actually preventing and repairing it.

Along the way I have authored three books: "10 Simple Solutions to Stress" (2007 New Harbinger) and "The Complete Idiot's Guide to Belly Fat Weight Loss" (2012 Alpha Books a division of Penguin) and "Pocket Therapy for Stress (2020 New Harbinger). Pocket Therapy is a re-release of my first book. I continue to offer workshops webinars and speeches both paid and as community service.

about:
I'm the founder and CEO of Inference Analytics AI where we're building one of the most trusted AI platforms for sensitive data and mission-critical workflows. From hospitals to manufacturing floors and global supply chains our goal is to put intelligent agentic AI in the hands of the people making high-stakes decisions every day.

I'm also the founder of Inferch.ai — a next-generation multi-model AI platform built around a simple but powerful idea: Synthesize intelligence. Query multiple models compare perspectives and receive a verified consensus. In a world where no single AI model has all the answers Inferch.ai brings together the best of them — helping users cut through noise reduce hallucinations and arrive at more reliable well-rounded intelligence.

I believe we are living through the most consequential moment in the history of AI. Large Language Models have evolved from impressive novelties into foundational infrastructure — powering autonomous agents multi-step reasoning real-time decision support and enterprise-grade workflows at scale. We're no longer asking "can AI do this?" — we're asking "how do we deploy it responsibly at speed and with trust?" That question drives everything I build. At Inference Analytics AI we're delivering secure enterprise-ready AI that doesn't just sound smart it performs where it matters most: in regulated high-stakes environments where failure is not an option.

We were early. Back in 2019 long before ChatGPT or foundation models became household names we published research on AI-powered text generation for clinical use cases in IEEE. Since then we've built a platform used by over 2 000 professionals across hospitals and are now scaling into manufacturing life sciences and other regulated industries where trust compliance and performance matter.

Before founding these ventures I led product and business units at some of the biggest names in data and analytics. I was VP and GM for IBM's Distribution Sector and earlier served as VP of Products at IRI. Earlier in my career I was a product manager at Informatica corp and MicroStrategy and kicked off my career at McKinsey & Company as a Technology Specialist. 

I have a Bachelor's in Electrical Engineering and Physics from Columbia University and an MBA from Kellogg School of Management at Northwestern. But most of what I've learned has come from building things scaling teams and working with some of the most demanding and inspiring industries in the world.

Across both ventures my mission is the same: make AI work where it matters most.
current_position_description: I lead Inference Analytics an enterprise AI company building secure compliant AI products and services for regulated industries—where privacy security and governance are non-negotiable. We develop both a no-code agentic AI platform that enables teams to design deploy and govern AI agents and workflows as well as production-grade agentic applications for real business and operational use cases. Our team brings deep end-to-end experience across the AI stack (machine learning NLP information extraction predictive analytics and generative AI) while we leverage LLMs when they're the right tool our text analytics and language understanding work pre-dates the current LLM era. We're deployed across leading healthcare systems where HIPAA compliance is foundational and in telecom environments with SOC-aligned controls—helping enterprises operationalize AI safely and effectively at scale.

about:
I’ve always been passionate about business and entrepreneurship so when both sets of my 
 twins had issues digesting their infant formulas I immediately got to work to create a gentler solution. 

My first set of twins Emory and Eden were born conjoined at 36 weeks in 2016. They passed away in the Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU) at 6 weeks old. 3 years later I gave birth to my sons. It was an emergency c-section at 35 weeks due to my uterus nearly rupturing. With my sons in the NICU it was nearly impossible to carry out my plan to breastfeed since I wasn’t making enough milk and due to their formula they started to develop severe digestive issues that were horrific to watch. This pain is what led me to develop Bunnee and improve the lack of quality inclusive infant formulas.

Bunnee is at the intersection of inclusive hypoallergenic formulas for sensitive littles and tech for parents - the ultimate solution to help the whole family navigate early stage nutrition without the headache…or the tummy-ache.

Besides being an entrepreneur and well basically superwoman (the whole two-time twin mom thing haha) I'm also a wife to my incredible husband Xavier an aspiring author a master target shopper and believe it or not a shower musician. 

On a relaxing non-work day you can find me writing raps humming as I make vegan desserts or binging Netflix. Among those things I love spending time with my family reading a great book listening to podcasts or drooling over home decor.

Right now I'm looking to create more genuine connections. Send me a message and share your story with me!
headline: Founder CEO at Bunnee the infant formula tech brand on a mission to save sensitive babies and stressed parents.

company_description:
For over 200 years the basic role of the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) has remained the same: to promote the progress of science and the useful arts by securing for limited times to inventors the exclusive right to their respective discoveries (Article 1 Section 8 of the United States Constitution). Under this system of protection American industry has flourished. New products have been invented new uses for old ones discovered and employment opportunities created for millions of Americans. The strength and vitality of the U.S. economy depends directly on effective mechanisms that protect new ideas and investments in innovation and creativity. The continued demand for patents and trademarks underscores the ingenuity of American inventors and entrepreneurs. The USPTO is at the cutting edge of the Nation's technological progress and achievement. 

The USPTO is a federal agency in the Department of Commerce. The USPTO occupies several interconnected buildings in Alexandria Virginia. The office employs more than 14 000 people -- including engineers scientists attorneys analysts computer specialists -- all dedicated to protecting U.S. intellectual property rights. At the USPTO the talents of all our colleagues enrich our vibrant workplace and we celebrate and value each one.

The founders of Duality myself included have been personally affected by the safety issues related to patient identification which has led to pain suffering and the death of our loved ones. 

At Duality we believe that good health data useful health data starts with accurate patient identification and we're on a passionate mission to help enable an interoperable health data ecosystem.

Mission: Accurately identify and link individual patients with their digitized health care information to enable an interoperable health data ecosystem.

about: Jake Becraft is a synthetic biologist and biotech entrepreneur as well as the co-founder and CEO of Strand Therapeutics. Strand is situated at the intersection of synthetic biology immunotherapy and mRNA therapeutics. Jake started his career developing the SAfER platform at MIT the world’s first synthetic biology programming language for mRNA. Beyond that work his broader interests span synthetic biology the intersection of hard-tech and biologically engineered therapeutics and biologically engineered organism-machine interfaces. He is an inventor on a multitude of patents in the biotech/gene therapy space and has won numerous awards for his scientific and entrepreneurial achievements including a Goldwater Scholarship a Viterbi Fellowship and an Amgen fellowship. He is an advocate among the life science entrepreneurial ecosystem for supporting founder-driven biotech startups and works to help increase young technical founders in biotech entrepreneurship. Jake has previously served as a Science and Technology advisor to legislators in the Massachusetts State Legislature. He often speaks internationally on the field of synthetic biology as well as the broader entrepreneurship environment necessary to bring next generation synbio gene therapeutics to market. Jake also serves on the Executive Board of Public Health United a non-profit focused on educating scientists across disciplines to foster their communication skills in order to become their own scientific ambassadors. He received his PhD in Biological Engineering and Synthetic Biology from MIT and his BS in Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign graduating Magna cum Laude with distinction. In his free time he is an avid back-country snowboarder and trains Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu.
company_description:
Strand was founded by biological engineers working together at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT). Building on the idea of creating smart therapies capable of making sophisticated decisions they sought to apply this concept to the emerging field of mRNA therapeutics. This area was previously untapped by traditional synthetic biology leading us to build our very own mRNA programming language creating the world's first platform for mRNA smart therapies. 

Strand's mRNA programming technology promises to make mRNA therapies safer and more effective by programming the location timing and intensity of therapeutic protein expression inside a patient's body via mRNA-encoded logic circuits. These circuits have the ability to implement cell-type specific expressions by sensing and classifying the unique mRNA expression signatures of cells and control the dosage of protein expression through responses to exogenously administered small molecules.
headline: Making medicine move faster Synthetic Biologist Co-Founder at Strand Therapeutics
